title: Chatterbox #344 вҖ“ The Ick
summary: Have you ever been on a great date, only to suddenly feel completely turned off by something small and unexpected? In this Chatterbox episode, Andrew and Anna talk about вҖңthe ick,вҖқ that sudden feeling of disgust toward someone youвҖҷre dating. They share their own icks, from almond milk to vocal fry, and look at why these reactions happen. Are they silly snap judgments, or could they actually be rooted in something deeper, like evolution or personal values? Practice using the expressions from this episode in your own conversations, and youвҖҷll sound more natural in no time. вҖ“ Andrew references articles from Time and Psychology Today: in the episode. title: Haiku in English 2 рҹҚӮвңҚпёҸ [982]
podcast_name: Luke's ENGLISH Podcast - Learn British English with Luke Thompson
summary: Haiku poems communicate a lot of ideas, thoughts and feelings into just a few simple words. They can be moving, humourous or meditative, and reading haiku in English allows you to learn vocabulary in a very interesting way. In part 1 I talked about the form and history of haiku, and read out various short poems in English from both Japanese writers and modern English ones. In part 2 here I finish reading my haiku selection, including many written by English-language haiku master John Stevenson. Listen to each one, consider the different meanings and interpretations and also learn specific words and phrases to expand your English vocabulary. title: Our World in English: Fighting child marriage through football
summary: Though illegal, child marriage is widely practiced in India. UNICEFвҖҷs latest estimate is that 216 million girls and women in India today have been married before they turned 18. But some girls and young women are trying to resist the practice. In this short documentary, we meet Munna, who found an unusual way out of becoming a child bride - through playing football. This episode of is based on the documentary India's sportswomen playing to be seen from Assignment on the BBC World Service. The original programme was reported by Divya Arya and produced by John Murphy.  Find the original programme here https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/w3ct6rcv  Get our latest programmes. title: Scenes Season 2 | Chapter 10: Un sueГұo hecho realidad
summary: Welcome back to ErnaвҖҷs CafГ© - our charming Spanish cafГ©, where the aroma of fresh coffee lingers, conversations flow, and life unfolds around every table. In this immersive series, we follow a compelling story set in a local cafГ© and help you build your language skills naturally through storytelling. The final chapter of Scenes for this season is here, and itвҖҷs a moment of pure joy and excitement. In Un sueГұo hecho realidad, Isabel reflects on a beautiful spring day filled with energy and anticipation. The cafГ© is buzzing as the regulars come together to support one of their own, and the evening holds a special surprise for everyone. El sol brilla y el ambiente en el bar estГЎ cargado de emociГіn. La terraza se llena de risas, buena compaГұГӯa y una sensaciГіn de que algo muy esperado estГЎ a punto de suceder. Con la llegada de la noche, todos estГЎn reunidos para vivir una velada que, sin saberlo, quedarГЎ en sus recuerdos. Come in and experience Un sueГұo hecho realidad. Want to take your learning further? Click here to access support materials and get more out of each chapter.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Scenes Season 2 | Chapter 9: La primavera y sus amores
summary: Welcome back to ErnaвҖҷs CafГ© - our charming Spanish cafГ©, where the aroma of fresh coffee lingers, conversations flow, and life unfolds around every table. In this immersive series, we follow a compelling story set in a local cafГ© and help you build your language skills naturally through storytelling. The latest chapter of Scenes is here and this one captures a moment thatвҖҷs as sweet as it is fleeting. In La primavera y sus amores, Isabel watches from a distance as young love quietly blossoms in the cafГ©. With the last of the spring celebrations fading and the day winding down, a chance encounter brings a special moment of connection. ItвҖҷs impossible not to be swept up in the magic of it all. La tarde se va apagando y la luz de la primavera sigue llenando el ambiente. En el bar, entre risas y despedidas de Гәltima hora, una joven parece esperar algoвҖҰ o mГЎs bien, a alguien. En un rincГіn tranquilo, dos miradas se cruzan y lo que comienza con una sonrisa tГӯmida se convierte en una conversaciГіn llena de emociГіn. La primavera, siempre tan impredecible, trae consigo momentos Гәnicos, como este. Step into La primavera y sus amores and enjoy the episode! Want to take your learning further? title: Simplified Speech #244 вҖ“ Using AI for personal messages
summary: Would you use AI to write a birthday message to your best friend? What about a condolence note when someone loses a parent? Inspired by this Guardian article, Andrew and Indiana share their honest opinions on a question a lot of people are asking: is it ever OK to let AI speak for you in your personal life? YouвҖҷll learn some useful expressions along the way and probably finish with a stronger opinion than you started with. Listening to real conversations between native speakers is one of the best ways to build your fluency and feel more confident in English. summary: En 2026, l'utilisation de l'IA est largement diffusГ©e, dans la vie professionnelle comme personnelle. La Fabrique Г  Idiots (vidГ©o YouTube) Transcript Intro Judith: [0:17] Bonjour HГ©lГЁne. HГ©lГЁne: [0:18] Salut Judith, comment Г§a va aujourd'hui ? Judith: [0:21] ГҮa va et toi ? HГ©lГЁne: [0:22] TrГЁs bien. Judith: [0:23] Est-ce qu'il pleut chez toi ? HГ©lГЁne: [0:24] Il pleut, il y a du brouillard, du vent, tout ce qu'on aime. Judith: [0:29] VoilГ , c'est un peu pareil, c'est un peu l'orage ici. HГ©lГЁne: [0:32] Ouais, donc un temps parfait pour enregistrer un Г©pisode de podcast. Judith: [0:36] Exactement. Et cette semaine, on va encore parler de l'IA. TrГЁs probablement qu'on n'aura jamais vraiment fini d'en parler, mais depuis la derniГЁre fois qu'on en a parlГ©, j'ai pas mal changГ© d'avis. HГ©lГЁne: [0:49] Ah, Г§a m'intГ©resse. Judith: [0:51] Ouais, et aussi dans cette logique de prendre le contre-pied de l'IA, on a une nouvelle pour vous les amis. Sachez que le 4 avril, Г  Paris, on organise une masterclass autour du thГЁme de la gastronomie. Ce sera autour d'un dГ©jeuner qui sera prГ©parГ© par une chef spГ©cialiste de la gastronomie franГ§aise traditionnelle. Et qu'est-ce qu'on va faire pendant cette masterclass, HГ©lГЁne ? HГ©lГЁne: [1:16] Alors, on va bien Г©videmment manger, mais aussi faire des jeux brise-glace pour apprendre Г  mieux se connaГ®tre pour parler franГ§ais. Judith: [1:40] Bref, le 4 avril, Г  Paris, on se voit en vrai et on apprend le franГ§ais en rencontrant des autres ГӘtres humains, en se faisant des amis. Bref, Г§a va ГӘtre trГЁs chouette. title: Scenes Season 2 | Chapter 9: Sommersonne und AbschiedslГӨcheln
summary: Welcome back to ErnaвҖҷs CafГ© - our charming German cafГ©, where the aroma of fresh coffee lingers, conversations flow, and life unfolds around every table. In this immersive series, we follow a compelling story set in a local cafГ© and help you build your language skills naturally through storytelling. Today, Felix works his final shift before starting a new job. Die Terrasse ist voll, die Sonne strahlt und Felix manГ¶vriert geschickt zwischen den Tischen. Erna behГӨlt alles im Blick und gibt kleine Anweisungen, wГӨhrend die ErzГӨhlerin Kuchen zu den GГӨsten bringt und zwei Jugendliche auf Rollschuhen vorbeisausen. Abschiede, Lachen und sommerliche Energie vermischen sich zu einem lebhaften Vormittag. This chapter is about farewells, busy mornings, and enjoying the moment. Want to take your learning further? Click here to access support materials and get more out of each chapter.  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: News in Slow German - #505 - Easy German Conversation about Current Events
summary: Wie immer beginnen wir unser Programm mit einem RГјckblick auf einige aktuelle Ereignisse. Der Krieg im Iran verschГӨrft sich. Heute betrachten wir einen besonderen Aspekt des Konflikts, nГӨmlichdie Auswirkungen des Krieges auf die globalen EnergiemГӨrkte, und wer davon profitieren kГ¶nnte. AnschlieГҹend sprechen wir Гјber Frankreichs AnkГјndigung einer neuen Nukleardoktrin. Frankreich rГјckt von seiner langjГӨhrigen Politik der вҖһstrikten Suffizienz" ab. Stattdessen will das Land seine AtomwaffenbestГӨnde aufstocken. In unserem Wissenschaftsteil diskutieren wir heute eine neue Studie, die zeigt, dass der globale Meeresspiegel aufgrund von MГӨngeln in den bisher verwendetenModellierungsmethoden deutlich unterschГӨtzt wurde. Und wir beenden den ersten Teil unseres Programms mit einer Diskussion Гјber die Entscheidung, Russland die RГјckkehr zur Biennale in Venedig zu erlauben. Diese Entscheidung hat erwartungsgemГӨГҹ groГҹe EmpГ¶rung ausgelГ¶st.    Der Rest des Programms ist der deutschen Sprache und Kultur gewidmet. Die heutige Grammatiklektion konzentriert sich auf Expressions with Machen вҖ“ Part 1. In Bayern gibt es eine Statistik, die jedes Jahr die Namen aller in diesem Bundesland lebenden MilchkГјhe erfasst. So kann man sehen, welche Namen besonders beliebt sind und wie kurios die Namensgebung manchmal ist. Denn hier sind der Fantasie keine Grenzen gesetzt. (Pause, Themenwechsel) Die Redewendungdieser Woche ist Das Wasser im Munde zusammenlaufen. Zu diesem Thema fallen einem auf Anhieb die deutsch-italienischen Eisdielen in Deutschland ein. Sie sind seit Jahrzehnten eine kulturelle Institution. Hier lГӨuft wirklich jedem das Wasser im Munde zusammen.     Die Angriffe der USA und Israels auf den Iran sorgen fГјr Turbulenzen auf den globalen EnergiemГӨrkten Frankreich kГјndigt neue Nukleardoktrin und Aufstockung von AtomwaffenbestГӨnden an Neue Studie: Meeresspiegel viel hГ¶her als bisher angenommen Russlands Biennale-Comeback lГ¶st EmpГ¶rung aus Beliebte und kuriose Kuh-Namen Bayerns Das deutsch-italienische EiscafГ© вҖ“ eine Institution

title: News in Slow Spanish
summary: Como siempre, comenzaremos el programa discutiendo la actualidad. En IrГЎn continГәa la guerra. Hoy analizaremos un aspecto particular del conflicto: su impacto sobre los mercados globales de la energГӯa, y quiГ©nes podrГӯan beneficiarse de ello. Continuaremos con el anuncio de una nueva doctrina nuclear en Francia, que abandona su tradicional polГӯtica de la "estricta suficiencia". En lugar de ello, bajo la nueva doctrina, Francia aumentarГЎ su arsenal nuclear. En el segmento de ciencia, comentaremos un nuevo estudio que indica que se ha subestimado considerablemente el aumento del nivel del mar, a causa de errores en los mГ©todos de modelizaciГіn utilizados con anterioridad. Concluiremos la discusiГіn de la actualidad con la decisiГіn de permitir el retorno de Rusia a la Bienal de Venecia. Como se esperaba, la decisiГіn ha provocado una gran indignaciГіn.    El resto del episodio de hoy lo dedicaremos a la lengua y la cultura espaГұolas. La primera conversaciГіn incluirГЎ ejemplos del tema de gramГЎtica de la semana, Adverbs of Quantity or Degree. En esta conversaciГіn hablaremos sobre la cesta de la compra en EspaГұa, cuyos precios suben cada dГӯa sin parar. La cesta de la compra es precisamente el indicador que usa el gobierno espaГұol para calcular el ГҚndice de Precios de Consumo. Discutiremos tambiГ©n posibles soluciones para parar esta subida constante. Y, en nuestra Гәltima conversaciГіn, aprenderemos a usar una nueva expresiГіn espaГұola, Cambiar de chaqueta o ser un chaquetero. La usaremos para hablar del teletrabajo en EspaГұa, regulado por una normativa del aГұo 2021. Las estadГӯsticas muestran que aunque el teletrabajo es una opciГіn para muchas empresas espaГұolas, el porcentaje de teletrabajadores no es tan alto como en la mayorГӯa de los paГӯses europeos alrededor de EspaГұa.   Los ataques de EE. UU. e Israel sobre IrГЎn tienen fuertes repercusiones en los mercados energГ©ticos mundiales Francia anuncia una nueva doctrina nuclear y un aumento del arsenal nuclear Un nuevo estudio indica que el nivel del mar ha subido mucho mГЎs de lo previamente estimado El retorno de Rusia a la Bienal de Venecia provoca indignaciГіn Adverbs of Quantity or Degree - La cesta de la compra, cada vez mГЎs cara Cambiar de chaqueta o ser un chaquetero - Teletrabajo en EspaГұa

title: Catch Word #292 вҖ“ Stick to your guns
summary: In this Catch Word lesson, Andrew and Indiana teach you two useful English idioms: вҖңstick to your gunsвҖқ and вҖңdouble down.вҖқ Both expressions describe a person who refuses to change their mind, even when they face pressure or criticism. They discuss the small differences between these idioms, explaining why one is often seen as a sign of respect while the other is usually considered stubborn. By listening to their conversation, you will learn how to use these idioms naturally in your own everyday speaking.В  The Best Way to Learn with This Episode: Culips members get an interactive transcript, helpful study guide, and ad-free audio for this episode. Take your English to the next level by becoming a Culips member. Become a Culips member now: Click here. Members can access the ad-free version here: Click here. Join our Discord community to connect with other learners and get more English practice. Click here to join. Fan Fact: Many popular English idioms actually come from the world of gambling and card games. For example, the phrase вҖңdouble downвҖқ comes from the game of Blackjack, where a player can double their bet in exchange for one more card. Another common expression mentioned in this episode is вҖңto fold,вҖқ which comes from Poker. In Poker, if you have a weak hand and donвҖҷt want to keep betting, you fold your cards and give up on that round. Expression list: to stick to oneвҖҷs gunsВ  to double downВ  to have oneвҖҷs mind made upВ  [Even] in the face ofВ  Peer pressureВ  To back down

title: #367 I Have a Big Confession to Make
summary: We'll be looking at the popular subreddit r/Confessions and for each story, I'll be sharing my own views on what this person should have done and giving them advice. While giving advice, I'll be using some useful structures that you can use in your English too.  Of course, we also learn a lot of bonus vocabulary that comes up in each story as well. If you wait around to the end, you'll even hear a couple of my own confessions where I talk about some bad things I've done (and still do!) title: 315. ItвҖҷs Not Just You: The Reality of Speaking English at Work

summary: Welcome to ВЎCuГ©ntame!, the podcast that helps you practice and improve your Spanish through stories from the Spanish-speaking world. In this episode, Marta shares a very special story. title: Moving to the UK?: UKвҖҷs New Immigration Policies Explained! (English Vocabulary Lesson)
summary: Is it becoming harder to move to a new country? Everywhere seems to be tightening the rules. Canada, the US, Australia, Europe, Japan, the UK. Countries that once focused on attracting people are now focused on control, limits, and conditions. And these changes are happening fast. If youвҖҷre thinking about moving to the UK, already living in the UK, or interested in how the world and immigration is changing, I think this episode matters to you. Because whatвҖҷs happening in British immigration policy is part of a much bigger global shift. Today, weвҖҷre going to break down the UKвҖҷs new immigration plans. WeвҖҷll talk about whatвҖҷs changing, who will be affected, and why these rules are becoming stricter. As this is Thinking in English, IвҖҷll avoid legal jargon, explain everything clearly, and highlight the key vocabulary as we go, so you can understand the policy and improve your English at the same time. title: Mi viaje a MГ©xico, parte 2
summary: In June of 2025 I finally traveled to a place I have wanted to visit for some time: Mexico. My trip to Mexico began with the beach and then the impressive ruins of ChichГ©n ItzГЎ. However, the best moments of the trip were yet to come, including a magical cenote and a more intimate experience with beautiful Mayan ruins. This story is in the first person and past tense. Important vocabulary in the story includes: вҖңcenoteвҖқ (spring fed lake), вҖңpiedraвҖқ (stone), and вҖңedificioвҖқ (building). No matter where you are in your language journey, stories will help you on your way. You can find a transcript of the story and read along at https://smalltownspanishteacher.com/2026/03/09/simple-stories-in-spanish-mi-viaje-a-mexico-parte-2/В  ВЎMuchas gracias por escuchar! summary: Welcome to part 2 of a series of English lessons about describing people. In this English class I will help you learn words we use to describe people. I'll be sure to help you learn the pronunciation of the word, its meaning, and I will give lots of example sentences that you can use in your next conversation. In this English lesson you'll learn words like: shy, talented, unpleasant, respected, observant, quiet, outspoken, ambitious, artistic, blunt, brilliant, competent, considerate, dramatic, distant, eager, fussy, impulsive, and many more. After watching this English lesson you'll be able to have even better English conversations as you describe people.

title: 211: Un festival corto, cortissimo!
podcast_name: Easy Italian: Learn Italian with real conversations | Imparare l'italiano con conversazioni reali
summary: Non proprio. Questo festival ГЁ stato un po'...  noioso. Ma ne parliamo bene con Raffaele, ormai super esperto di festival. E io (Matteo), no, non ho visto il festival ma ho ascoltato alcune canzoni! Incresibile... Trascrizione interattiva e Vocab Helper Support Easy Italian and get interactive transcripts, live vocabulary and bonus content: easyitalian.fm/membership Note dell'episodio Is Lake Como Worth Visiting? We Asked the Locals - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7JBs2wPV2eE Ma che strano questo festival di Sanremo! Breve? No in realtГ  ГЁ durato come sempre circa una settimana. Questa volta perГІ in Italia se ne ГЁ parlato solo per una settimana o poco piГ№, mentre di solito se ne parla per almeno un mese prima e qualche giorno dopo. Ma chi ha vinto questa edizione del festival? Sal Da Vinci https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sal_Da_Vinci Questa volta ho ascoltato, non visto adesso non esageriamo, alcune canzoni e mi pare siano state tutte o quasi tutte canzoni d'amore, e insomma, un po' troppo. Anche Raffaele ha notato la prevalenza del tema dell'amore. Ma quali sono piaciute a me, e quali a Raffaele? Abbiamo poi parlato di due artisti con nomi strani: Ditonellapiaga https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ditonellapiaga TonyPitony https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/TonyPitony Concludiamo con una notiziona per il prossimo festival.  Stefano De Martino sarГ  il nuovo conduttore del festival di Sanremo 2027! https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stefano_De_Martino Ma come andrГ ? Io no sono fiduciosissimo e Raffaele cercherГ  di convicermi del contrario nell'aftershow. Curiosi? Per avere l'accesso all'aftershow basta essere membri della comunitГ  Easy Italian! easyitalian.fm/membership Trascrizione Matteo: [0:23] Buongiorno. Raffaele: [0:25] Buongiorno Matteo, benvenuti a marzo. Matteo: [0:32] Ben... quasi "primaverati". No, non si dice, non esiste questa parola. Raffaele: [0:38] "Primaverati" ГЁ la prima volta che la sento. Matteo: [0:41] PerchГ© uno "si primavera" quando entra nella primavera. Raffaele: [0:45] Quindi adesso in italiano funziona che possiamo creare i verbi come l'inglese? Matteo: [0:49] Ma sГ¬... ... title: #85 Ces mots que tu apprends mais qu'on ne dit (presque) jamais
summary: рҹҺҘ Pour la premiГЁre fois, on a filmГ© un Г©pisode du podcast ! On est super heureux. La version vidГ©o est Г  dГ©couvrir sur notre chaГ®ne YouTube principale. On serait ravis d'avoir ton avis :) -------------------------------------------- Dans cet Г©pisode, on parle des mots que tu connais dГ©jГ вҖҰ mais que les FranГ§ais ne disent presque jamais. Des mots de grammaire, des connecteurs, du vocabulaire niveau B1/B2 quвҖҷon voit partout dans les manuels, mais quвҖҷon nвҖҷentend presque jamais dans une vraie conversation. On te donne des exemples concrets, trГЁs connus, et on les compare avec ce quвҖҷon dit vraiment Г  lвҖҷoral. Dans la deuxiГЁme partie, on parle aussi du vocabulaire des mГ©dias : ces mots que tu entends aux informations et que tu comprends. Mais qui sonnent bizarrement si tu les utilises dans la vie quotidienne. On nвҖҷest pas toujours dвҖҷaccord, alors on dГ©bat, on rГ©flГ©chit et surtout, on rГ©pond Г  ces deux questions importantes : Est-ce quвҖҷil faut quand mГӘme apprendre et utiliser ces mots ? Et pourquoi les professeurs les enseignent si les natifs ne les utilisent presque pas ? Un Г©pisode trГЁs concret mais aussi une vraie rГ©flexion sur la diffГ©rence entre le franГ§ais des livres et le franГ§ais vivant. вҶ’ La transcription complГЁte et dГ©taillГ©e de cet Г©pisode est disponible ici.  ------------------------------------ En t'abonnant Г  notre Patreon, tu auras accГЁs Г  du contenu exclusif. title: News In Slow French #784- Intermediate French Weekly Program
summary: Nous commencerons notre discussion surВ l'actualitГ©В par une conversation sur le refus d'Anthropic d'accepter les conditions du Pentagone concernant l'utilisation militaire de son chatbot Claude.В Nous poursuivronsВ en commentantВ une nouvelle Г©tudeВ quiВ montreВ qu'un rГ©seau social peut trГЁs rapidementВ faire basculerВ les opinions politiques d'une personne versВ la droite.В La discussion suivanteВ portera surВ une Г©tude publiГ©e dans la revue Science quiВ expliqueВ pourquoi l'ascendance nГ©andertalienne prГ©sente chez les humains aujourd'hui est rГ©partieВ de maniГЁre inГ©galeВ dans le gГ©nome. Et enfin, nous cГ©lГ©brerons la Semaine nationale de la procrastination aux Гүtats-Unis.  В   Le reste de l'Г©mission d'aujourd'hui sera consacrГ© Г  la langue et Г  la culture franГ§aises. Notre point de grammaire de la semaine sera :В Prepositional Phrases du cГҙtГ© de, en dehors de, en dГ©pit de, en face de, en plus de, en raison de, and Г©tant donnГ©. Nous nous intГ©resserons Г  la 62ГЁmeВ Г©dition du Salon de l'agriculture, qui a Г©tГ© marquГ©e parВ une forte baisseВ du nombre de visiteurs. Nous verrons quel effet a eu l'absence de vaches. Et nous terminerons avec l'expression de la semaine,В Mettre les points sur les i. Nous parlerons des vingt ans duВ dispositifВ Alerte enlГЁvement, qui a permis de retrouverВ sains et saufsВ de nombreux enfantsВ enlevГ©s.  - Anthropic et le PentagoneВ en dГ©saccordВ surВ l'utilisationВ de l'IA dans le domaine de la dГ©fense et de la sГ©curitГ©  - Une Г©tudeВ rГ©vГЁle comment les algorithmes de X modifient les opinions politiques desВ utilisateurs  - GГ©nГ©tique : de nouvelles hypothГЁses surВ les modes de reproductionВ des NГ©andertaliens et des humains  - Le Procrastinators' Club of America cГ©lГЁbre la Semaine nationale de la procrastination  - Le Salon de l'agricultureВ connaГ®tВ une baisse de frГ©quentationВ spectaculaire  - Le dispositif В« Alerte enlГЁvement В» a 20 ans

title: News In Slow Italian #686- Intermediate Italian Weekly Program
summary: ApriamoВ la nostraВ rassegna di attualitГ В con una conversazione sulВ rifiutoВ da parte di Anthropic delle condizioniВ imposte dalВ Pentagono per l'uso letale del suo chatbot Claude.В ProseguiamoВ con un nuovo studio cheВ mostra comeВ una piattaforma socialeВ possaВ spostare molto rapidamenteВ le opinioni politiche di una personaВ verso destra. La nostra prossima discussioneВ riguardaВ uno studio pubblicato sullaВ rivistaВ ScienceВ cheВ spiegaВ perchГ©В l'ereditГ  geneticaВ dei Neanderthal presente negliВ esseri umaniВ di oggiВ sia distribuitaВ in modo disomogeneoВ nel genoma. E infine,В celebreremoВ la Settimana Nazionale della ProcrastinazioneВ negli Stati Uniti.  В   La seconda parte di questa puntata ГЁ dedicata alla lingua e alla cultura italiana.В L'argomento grammaticaleВ di oggi ГЁВ Expressing Agreement and Disagreement.В Ne trovereteВ diversi esempi nel dialogo in cui si discute di un'importanteВ opera d'arteВ acquistataВ dallo Stato italiano tramite la prestigiosaВ casa d'asteВ Sotheby's di New York, anticipando altri potenzialiВ acquirenti.В Il dipintoВ in questione ГЁ un "Ecce Homo" di Antonello da Messina. Nel finaleВ ci soffermeremo sull'espressione idiomatica di oggi:В Mettere il naso fuori di casa. La ritroverete nel dialogo dedicato al Carnevale di Venezia,В il piГ№ celebreВ d'Italia. Ogni annoВ richiama centinaiaВ diВ migliaiaВ di turisti eВ riempieВ la cittГ  di colori e maschere. Una festaВ affollatissimaВ che, paradossalmente,В appartieneВ sempre menoВ ai suoi abitanti.  - Anthropic e il Pentagono inВ disaccordoВ sull'uso dell'IA nella difesa e nella sicurezza  - Uno studio rivela come gli algoritmi di X influenzano le opinioni politiche degliВ utenti  - I genetisti ipotizzano suiВ modelli diВ accoppiamentoВ tra Neanderthal e Homo sapiens  - Il Procrastinators' Club of America celebra la Settimana Nazionale della Procrastinazione  - Ecce homo:В il capolavoroВ che l'ItaliaВ si ГЁ ripresa  - Carnevale di Venezia: tra turismo di massa e cittГ  che si svuota

title: Simplified Speech #243 вҖ“ How we learn our second languages
summary: In this episode of Simplified Speech, Andrew and Indiana share their personal experiences as language learners. Even though they are English teachers, they also study second languages themselves. They talk about their history with languages like Japanese, German, and Korean, and discuss their favorite study methods and give advice on how to improve your skills.В  They chat about some of the different ways they remember new vocabulary and why spending time with the language every day is so important. Finally, they also share some funny and unusual tips for practicing speaking without feeling embarrassed. Listen to the episode to find out which daily habits might work best for you. title: News in Slow Spanish - #886 - Study Spanish While Listening to the News
summary: Comenzaremos la discusiГіn de la actualidad comentando el rechazo de Anthropic a los tГ©rminos del PentГЎgono para el uso letal de su chatbot Claude. Continuaremos con un estudio que demuestra que una red social puede provocar muy rГЎpidamente un giro a la derecha de la orientaciГіn polГӯtica de la gente. Nuestra siguiente discusiГіn trata sobre un estudio publicado en la revista Science, que explica por quГ© la ascendencia neandertal que estГЎ presente actualmente en los humanos estГЎ distribuГӯda de forma desigual en nuestro genoma. Y, para acabar, celebraremos la Semana Nacional de la ProcrastinaciГіn de EE. UU.  El resto del episodio de hoy lo dedicaremos a la lengua y la cultura espaГұolas. La primera conversaciГіn incluirГЎ ejemplos del tema de gramГЎtica de la semana, Adverbs of Mode and Manner. En esta conversaciГіn hablaremos sobre la transformaciГіn de la estructura del hogar en EspaГұa. En el siglo XXI, los hogares espaГұoles con un nГәcleo conyugal sin hijos se han triplicado, mientras que los hogares unipersonales se han quintuplicado! En algunos paГӯses europeos, las cosas son distintasвҖҰ Entonces, Вҝvivimos una crisis o un cambio demogrГЎficoвҖҰ? Y, en nuestra Гәltima conversaciГіn, aprenderemos a usar una nueva expresiГіn espaГұola, De cara a la galerГӯa. La usaremos para hablar del libro de memorias que escribiГі el rey emГ©rito, don Juan Carlos I. Este libro llegГі a las librerГӯas espaГұolas un mes despuГ©s de las librerГӯas francesas. Sin embargo, en EspaГұa, las ventas no fueron tan buenas como en Francia. El motivo es un poco dudoso y discutiremos el porquГ©.   Anthropic y el PentГЎgono discrepan en el uso de la IA para defensa y seguridad Un estudio muestra cГіmo los algoritmos de X alteran la opiniГіn polГӯtica de los usuarios Los genetistas especulan sobre los patrones de apareamiento de neandertales y humanos El Club de los Procrastinadores de AmГ©rica celebra la Semana Nacional de la ProcrastinaciГіn TransformaciГіn de la estructura del hogar en EspaГұa ReconciliaciГіn, el libro de memorias del rey emГ©rito

title: E133 ВҝComprarГӯas una Barbie a tu hijo? - Spanish for False Beginners
podcast_name: Spanish for False Beginners - EspaГұol para falsos principiantes
summary: En este podcast de espaГұol para estudiantes de espaГұol intermedio bajo o principiantes avanzados, todo empieza con una pregunta muy simple: ВҝtenГӯas Barbies cuando eras pequeГұo? Pero la conversaciГіn se complica rГЎpidamente. Hablamos de juguetes de nuestra infancia, de Action Man, de Disney y de por quГ© algunos padres dudaban en comprar ciertas muГұecas. TambiГ©n reflexionamos sobre estereotipos de gГ©nero, educaciГіn y la pelГӯcula de Barbie. Yo comparto algunas experiencias personales y El InglГ©s aporta una perspectiva britГЎnica bastante interesante. title: The Great Molasses Flood of 1919
summary: In this episode, we explore the Great Molasses Flood of 1919, one of the strangest and most tragic disasters in U.S. history. On a cold January afternoon in BostonвҖҷs North End, a massive steel tank holding 2.3 million gallons of molasses burst, sending a deadly wave of syrup through the streets. YouвҖҷll learn what molasses was being used for, why shipments kept arriving after World War I, and how Prohibition played a surprising role in the story. WeвҖҷll walk step by step through what caused the tank to fail,  and how this disaster helped shape modern building inspections and safety codes. Along the way, I explain key vocabulary likeВ seep, debris, dense, rivets, bootleg,В andВ up to codeВ вҖ” so youвҖҷll build your English while learning about U.S. history and culture. вӯҗ Get the full transcript, vocabulary list, quiz, and bonus lesson on the third conditional inside the Academy. title: History of Berlin! (English Vocabulary Lesson)
summary: Berlin is one of the most iconic cities in the world. Few cities have been destroyed so completely, divided so deeply, and then rebuilt so many times. Understanding BerlinвҖҷs past helps us understand the development of Germany and Europe. The rise of empires, the impact of nationalism, the horrors of totalitarianism, and the tension of the Cold War are all part of this cityвҖҷs history. BerlinвҖҷs story is not just local; it reflects some of the biggest political and social forces of the last 800 years. In this episode, weвҖҷll follow BerlinвҖҷs journey from its medieval beginnings, through its rise as the capital of a powerful empire, into a city torn in two during the Cold War, and finally to its reunification as the capital of a united Germany. And at the same time, we will learn some important and useful English vocabulary! title: Mi viaje a MГ©xico, parte 1
summary: In June of 2025 I finally traveled to a place I have wanted to visit for some time: Mexico. It was an amazing adventure filled with history, food, fun, and friendship. It inspired several story ideas, some of which you have already heard and will probably recognize, and some that are still in the works. Above all, this trip to Mexico reminded me of why I love to travel. This story is in the first person and past tense. Important vocabulary in the story includes: вҖңsolitarioвҖқ (lone), вҖңviajarвҖқ (travel), вҖңjamaicaвҖқ (hibiscus), and вҖңmolestabaвҖқ (bothered). No matter where you are in your language journey, stories will help you on your way. You can find a transcript of the story and read along at https://smalltownspanishteacher.com/2026/03/02/simple-stories-in-spanish-mi-viaje-a-mexico-parte-1/В  ВЎMuchas gracias por escuchar! Thank you for listening, and a HUGE thank you for your support. I really enjoy creating and sharing simple, comprehensible stories in Spanish. If you would like to help me in that endeavor, consider buying me a taco at https://www.buymeacoffee.com/SpanishTeacher . You can also find me on Venmo and PayPal @Small Town Spanish Teacher Support the show

title: Bonus episode #169 вҖ“ Stories from Canada
summary: Canada is famous for its cold winters, but what is it really like to visit in January? In this weekвҖҷs bonus episode, Andrew shares stories about his recent trip back to his hometown in Canada. He traveled there to enjoy the snow and do some classic winter activities. However, the weather didnвҖҷt exactly match his plans! You will hear about his experience ice skating high up in the mountains and his trip to watch a local ice hockey game that had a very wild and surprising moment. Listening to this story will help you improve your English fluency. You will also learn useful, everyday words about winter weather, sports, and Canadian culture. title: Let's Learn English! Topic: Describing People! рҹ§©рҹ‘‘рҹҸҲ
podcast_name: Learn English with Bob the Canadian
summary: In this English lesson I will help you learn words and phrases that can be used to describe people in English. These aren't easy words like short and tall, but rather more advance words we use to describe someone's personality and behaviour in English. Once you know these words, you'll be able to describe friends, family members, and colleagues with ease. In this English lesson you'll learn words and phrases like: absentminded, awkward, clumsy, cruel, arrogant, jealous, adorable, generous, warm, impatient, aggressive, polite, calm, vulgar, lovable, violent, funny, lazy, honest, dishonest, loyal, and many more! I hope you enjoy this free English class about describing people! Have a great day!

summary: Come sempre, la prima parte della puntata ГЁ dedicataВ all'attualitГ . Nel primo servizioВ parleremo dellaВ decisione della Francia di limitare l'accesso dell'ambasciatoreВ statunitenseВ ai ministri del governo. Poi,В commenteremoВ le dure critiche espresse da diversi leader europei sulВ sostegnoВ americano all'Ucraina. Da una prospettiva europea, l'abbandono dell'Ucraina da parte degli Stati Uniti di fronte all'aggressione russa equivale a un vero e proprioВ tradimento. Nella sezione scientifica parleremo dell'energia solare spaziale, cheВ potrebbe garantireВ elettricitГ  pulita e affidabile indipendentementeВ dal meteoВ o dall'alternarsi del giorno e della notte. EВ concluderemoВ la prima parte della puntata con una conversazione sul Festival del Cinema di Berlino,В travolto dalle polemicheВ perВ la sua gestioneВ delВ dibattitoВ politico. Nel finaleВ ci soffermeremoВ sull'espressione idiomatica di oggi:В Prendere Piede.В La ritrovereteВ nel dialogo dedicato al ritrovamento di alcuneВ fonti scritteВ cheВ potrebbero riscrivereВ la storia delle origini di uno deiВ piattiВ piГ№ celebri e discussi della tradizione culinaria italiana: la carbonara.  - La FranciaВ negaВ all'ambasciatoreВ statunitenseВ l'accesso ai ministri del governo  - Il tradimentoВ degli Stati UnitiВ nei confronti dell'Ucraina ГЁ in linea con il nuovoВ ordine politico mondiale  - L'energia solare dallo spazio puГІ garantire l'indipendenza energetica dell'Europa?  - La Berlinale 2026В oscurata dalle polemicheВ sul ruolo della politica nel cinema  - Il Giudizio UniversaleВ di MichelangeloВ torna sotto restauro  - Carbonara: una leggenda tutta da riscrivere

title: Simplified Speech # 242 вҖ“ Separating the art from the artist
summary: In this Simplified Speech episode, Andrew and Indiana discuss a difficult question: Can you separate the art from the artist? Sometimes, famous singers, writers, or actors do bad things or share opinions that hurt other people. Andrew and Indiana talk about if it is OK to still enjoy their music, books, or movies when this happens. By listening to this conversation, you will learn useful vocabulary to talk about controversy and personal values. This lesson will help you practice listening to natural English conversations and express your own thoughts about famous people who make bad choices. The Best Way to Learn with This Episode: Culips members get an interactive transcript, helpful study guide, and ad-free audio for this episode. Take your English to the next level by becoming a Culips member. Become a Culips member now: Click here. Members can access the ad-free version: Click here. Join our Discord community to connect with other learners and get more English practice. Click here to join. Fun Fact Did you know that the popular word вҖңcanceledвҖқ actually comes from a 1991 movie? In the film New Jack City, a gangster gets angry at his girlfriend and tells someone to вҖңcancelвҖқ her. Years later, people on social media started using this exact word to say they will stop supporting famous people who make bad choices. It shows how a simple movie line can grow into a word that everyone uses to talk about problematic artists today. Expression list problematicВ  separate the art from the artists clear-cutВ  blanket statementВ  to own up toВ  boycotting

title: News in Slow Spanish - #885 - Best Spanish Program for Intermediate Learners
summary: Como siempre, dedicaremos la primera parte del programa a comentar la actualidad. En la primera noticia, discutiremos la decisiГіn de Francia de retirarle al embajador estadounidense el acceso a los ministros del Gobierno. DespuГ©s, hablaremos de la visiГіn crГӯtica que tienen muchos lГӯderes europeos del apoyo de EE. UU. a Ucrania. Desde el punto de vista europeo, el abandono estadounidense de Ucrania, mientras esta se enfrenta a los ataques rusos, equivale a una traiciГіn. En el segmento de ciencia, hablaremos de la energГӯa solar espacial, que podrГӯa proporcionar electricidad de forma fiable y limpia con independencia de la meteorologГӯa o del momento del dГӯa. Y concluiremos la primera parte del programa de hoy comentando el Festival de Cine de BerlГӯn, que se vio sacudido por una polГ©mica sobre su enfoque en relaciГіn al discurso polГӯtico.  El resto del episodio de hoy lo dedicaremos a la lengua y la cultura espaГұolas. La primera conversaciГіn incluirГЎ ejemplos del tema de gramГЎtica de la semana, Adverbs of Place. En esta conversaciГіn hablaremos del mercado de la vivienda en EspaГұa donde la demanda supera la oferta y en consecuencia los precios se disparan. Revisaremos las posibles causas de este problema y ejemplos de paГӯses alrededor de EspaГұa con un problema parecido y cГіmo lo estГЎn solucionando. Y, en nuestra Гәltima conversaciГіn, aprenderemos a usar una nueva expresiГіn espaГұola, Ser peor el remedio que la enfermedad. La usaremos para hablar del uso del velo integral por algunas mujeres espaГұolas. Aunque EspaГұa es un estado aconfesional y las personas y las comunidades religiosas pueden practicar libremente segГәn sus creencias, nos preguntamos si el Gobierno deberГӯa prohibir esta manifestaciГіn religiosa, pues podrГӯa limitar el empoderamiento de las mujeres.   Francia le retira al embajador de EE. UU. el acceso a los ministros del Gobierno La traiciГіn estadounidense a Ucrania encaja con el nuevo orden polГӯtico mundial ВҝPuede la energГӯa solar espacial asegurar la independencia energГ©tica de Europa? La Berlinale 2026, ensombrecida por una pelea sobre la polГӯtica en el cine El mercado de la vivienda en EspaГұa EspaГұa, un estado aconfesional

title: E243 ВҝQuГ© hace a una persona? - EspaГұol Intermedio
summary: En este episodio del pГіdcast de espaГұol intermedio de Spanish Language Coach hablamos de una pregunta fascinante: ВҝquГ© hace a una persona? No hablamos solo de tu nombre o tu profesiГіn, sino de tu identidad, tu memoria, tu conciencia y tu cultura. ВҝSomos solo biologГӯa? ВҝSomos nuestros recuerdos? ВҝQuГ© pasa si cambiamos de paГӯs, de trabajo o incluso de idioma? A travГ©s de ejemplos claros y reflexiones sencillas, exploramos conceptos como identidad, conciencia, responsabilidad, libertad, emociones y narrativa personal. TambiГ©n hablamos del papel del lenguaje y de cГіmo aprender espaГұol puede cambiar tu forma de ver el mundo y ampliar tu identidad. title: #232: Words That Trigger the Present Progressive in Italian
summary: Italian uses the present progressive far less frequently than English and typically reserves it for actions happening right at the moment of speaking. In most other cases, the simple present tense is preferred. There are, however, specific time expressions that clearly signal when the progressive form should be used. title: Expression: In a Sticky Situation
summary: In this engaging (and slightly sticky) episode, we explore the common American English idiomВ вҖңto be in a sticky situation.вҖқВ After a real-life kitchen disaster involving spilled honey, we break down what the expression means, where it comes from (it dates back over 200 years!), and how Americans actually use it in everyday conversation. YouвҖҷll hear relatable examplesвҖ”from accidentally hitting вҖңReply AllвҖқ at work to public mix-ups at theВ Academy Awardsand even the New Coke decision byВ Coca-Cola. WeвҖҷll also compare similar expressions and practice pronunciation so you can say it naturally and confidently. title: Who was Alan Turing? (English Vocabulary Lesson)
summary: Every time you type a message, unlock your phone, or trust a computer to make a decision, youвҖҷre relying on the ideas of someone you may never have heard of and probably never learned about at school. That person is Alan Turing. He was a British mathematician, logician, and wartime codebreaker, and one of the most important figures in modern science and technology. Turing helped lay the foundations of modern computing. He played a crucial role in breaking Nazi codes during the Second World War. And he asked questions about machines that still shape how we think about artificial intelligence today. His influence is everywhere, from the security that protects your data to the algorithms behind AI. In this episode, I want to explore Alan TuringвҖҷs life, his scientific achievements, and the legacy he left behind, before connecting his story to my Greatest Scientist of All Time series. title: My Mum talks about her life [977]
podcast_name: Luke's ENGLISH Podcast - Learn British English with Luke Thompson
summary: [977] Recently I visited my parents at their home, and while I was there I took the time to record conversations with them both. This first one is with my mum. I asked her questions about her life story, our family history, her memories of growing up and so many other things that I was curious about. The result is this recording. I hope you will find that it is not only good for your English listening practice, but also fascinating and absorbing as a piece of personal history. Full transcript available. title: Bonus episode #168 вҖ“ Sick as a dog
summary: Traveling abroad is usually a fun adventure, but what happens when you get sick in a different country? In this weekвҖҷs bonus episode, Andrew shares a story about his recent trip to Tokyo. He traveled there to see a concert and meet Culips listeners. However, a sudden illness almost ruined his plans. You will hear about how he tried to finish his trip and his long journey back to South Korea to find a doctor. Listening to this story will help you improve your English fluency. You will also learn useful, everyday words about traveling, being sick, and getting better. summary: I'm not a Science expert, but I know enough to be able to teach an English lesson about it. Join me for an in depth look at the words and phrases we use when talking about Science in English! In this English lesson you'll learn words and phrases like: experiment, microscope, test tube, lab coat, telescope, magnifying glass, petri dish, goggles, bunsen burner, and many more! I hope you enjoy this free English class about Science!

title: News in Slow Spanish - #884 - Study Spanish While Listening to the News
summary: Comenzaremos nuestra discusiГіn de la actualidad con la Conferencia de Seguridad de MГәnich, que tuvo lugar el pasado fin de semana. DespuГ©s, hablaremos de las acusaciones contra el Kremlin de que envenenГі al destacado lГӯder opositor ruso AlexГ©i Navalni hace dos aГұos. La secciГіn de ciencia la dedicaremos a un estudio que asegura que la estimulaciГіn elГ©ctrica del cerebro puede hacer menos egoГӯsta a la gente. Y concluiremos la primera parte del programa comentando la iniciativa irlandesa de promover las artes ofreciendo una renta bГЎsica para 2.000 artistas durante un periodo de tres aГұos.  El resto del episodio de hoy lo dedicaremos a la lengua y la cultura espaГұolas. La primera conversaciГіn incluirГЎ ejemplos del tema de gramГЎtica de la semana, Adverbs of Time. En esta conversaciГіn hablaremos de un servicio de avisos de emergencias a travГ©s de los telГ©fonos mГіviles, el ES-Alert. EspaГұa dispone de este servicio desde el aГұo 2022. Se usa, por ejemplo, en alarmas de emergencia de predicciones meteorolГіgicas. Sin embargo, el uso que se le ha dado Гәltimamente a este servicio parece ser un poco excesivo. Y, en nuestra Гәltima conversaciГіn, aprenderemos a usar una nueva expresiГіn espaГұola, MГЎs se perdiГі en Cuba y volvieron cantando. La usaremos para hablar de la llamada generaciГіn perdida. Este tГ©rmino se usa para hablar de los espaГұoles nacidos entre 1985 y 1995, una generaciГіn acadГ©micamente muy bien preparada. Sin embargo, les cogiГі la crisis inmobiliaria cuando empezaron su vida laboral. DespuГ©s, una gran precariedad laboral, y finalmente, la pandemia. El problema para mucha gente de esta Г©poca es una gran desmotivaciГіn. Pero algunos tambiГ©n vieron oportunidadesвҖҰ   Europa recibe mensajes confusos de EE. UU. sobre las relaciones transatlГЎnticas La secretaria de Exteriores britГЎnica acusa al Kremlin de envenenar a AlexГ©i Navalni La estimulaciГіn elГ©ctrica del cerebro puede hacer mГЎs generosa a la gente Irlanda lanza una renta bГЎsica de tres aГұos de duraciГіn para artistas EnvГӯo de alertas a los telГ©fonos mГіviles GeneraciГіn Perdida en EspaГұa

title: #84 Ce que le journalisme nous a appris
summary: рҹҺҷпёҸ Si tu nous Г©coutes attentivement, tu le sais. Avant de crГ©er French With Panache, nous avons Г©tГ© tous les deux journalistes. Pendant plusieurs annГ©es, nous avons travaillГ© dans diffГ©rents mГ©dias en France, principalement Г  la radio. Dans cet Г©pisode, on te raconte cette ancienne vie : les rГ©dactions dans lesquelles nous avons travaillГ©, notre quotidien, les reportages que nous avons rГ©alisГ©s, les rencontres qui nous ont marquГ©s. Et les moments plus difficiles aussi. On tвҖҷexplique pourquoi nous avons quittГ© le journalisme, et surtout ce que ce mГ©tier nous a appris, humainement et professionnellement, avec du recul. Dans la deuxiГЁme partie de la conversation, on parle de la langue des mГ©dias franГ§ais : le franГ§ais quвҖҷon entend Г  la radio et Г  la tГ©lГ©vision est-il naturel ? Est-ce une bonne idГ©e de lвҖҷutiliser pour apprendre le franГ§ais ?  Cette fois, on te rГ©pond avec notre regard de professeurs. Et Г  la fin de lвҖҷГ©pisode, Nathan te recommande une radio trГЁs populaire en France, parfaite pour progresser en franГ§ais parlГ©, et plus familier.  ------------------------------------------ Pour obtenir les transcriptions de tous nos Г©pisodes et de nos vidГ©os, avoir accГЁs Г  des Г©pisodes exclusifs supplГ©mentaires, des activitГ©s de comprГ©hension, un petit groupe WhatsApp pour parler avec nous et des apprenants du monde entier, etc. Rejoins nous sur notre Patreon :  вҒ https://www.patreon.com/cw/FrenchWithPanacheвҒ вҒ вҒ  FOLLOW US :  YouTube : вҒ вҒ https://www.youtube.com/@FrenchWithPanacheвҒ вҒ  Instagram :вҒ вҒ  https://www.instagram.com/frenchwithpanache/вҒ вҒ  Private Coaching : вҒ вҒ https://frenchwithpanache.com/our-offers/private-coaching/вҒ вҒ  Our website : вҒ вҒ frenchwithpanache.comвҒ вҒ  Work with us : вҒ вҒ вҒ contact@frenchwithpanache.comвҒ вҒ вҒ  Support our content at : вҒ вҒ вҒ https://www.paypal.com/donate/?hosted_button_id=WTYFE2AY2YVX4вҒ вҒ вҒ  or вҒ вҒ вҒ https://ko-fi.com/frenchwithpanacheвҒ вҒ вҒ  ГҖ trГЁs vite рҹ’ҷ Violaine & Nathan

summary: ApriamoВ la nostraВ rassegna di attualitГ В conВ la Conferenza sulla sicurezza a Monaco, cheВ si ГЁ svoltaВ lo scorso fine settimana. PoiВ parleremoВ delle accuse rivolteВ al Cremlino perВ l'avvelenamento,В avvenutoВ due anniВ fa,В del notoВ leader dell'opposizione russa Alexei Navalny. title: Simplified Speech #241 вҖ“ Keeping secrets
summary: Can you keep a secret? In this episode, Andrew and Kassy talk about the difference between being an вҖңopen bookвҖқ and a вҖңclosed book.вҖқ They discuss their own habits for keeping things private, share funny stories about childhood crushes, and debate whether it is harder to keep good news or bad news a secret. The Best Way to Learn with This Episode: Culips members get an interactive transcript, helpful study guide, and ad-free audio for this episode. Join our Discord community to connect with other learners and get more English practice. Click here to join. Fun Fact Andrew mentions being вҖңin a pickle.вҖқ This is a funny way of saying you are in a difficult situation. While we usually think of pickles as a tasty snack, the phrase comes from the idea of being all mixed up or вҖңstewed,вҖқ just like vegetables in a jar of vinegar! title: 10 Common вҖңBigвҖқ Idioms YouвҖҷll Hear in Everyday English [575]
summary: рҹҡЁImprove your English with structured online lessons. Book a free trial and get clear feedback on your level, pronunciation and speaking. title: Do We Live in a Surveillance Society? вҖ“ Prepositions of Place (English Grammar Lesson)
summary: Grammar Study Pack - https://www.patreon.com/posts/prepositions-of-148657101?utm_medium=clipboard_copy&utm_source=copyLink&utm_campaign=postshare_creator&utm_content=join_link Last year, I was walking on the street back home in the UK, and I started noticing just how many security cameras there were around me. There were cameras on lampposts, cameras in shops, and cameras at train stations, quietly watching people come and go. Once you start looking for them, you see them everywhereвҖҰ. on the walls, in buildings, and at entrances. WhatвҖҷs interesting is that most of the time, we donвҖҷt even notice them. We walk in our cities, shop in stores, and commute at stations without really thinking about how much weвҖҷre being watched. Surveillance has become such a normal part of life that it fades into the background. So hereвҖҷs the question I want you to think about. When you walk in your city, how many cameras do you think are watching you? And do you feel safer because of them? or do they make you feel a little uncomfortable? In todayвҖҷs episode, weвҖҷre going to explore what it really means to live in a surveillance society. WeвҖҷll look at the benefits, at the risks, and at the ethical questions surrounding surveillance.В  At the same time, IвҖҷll help you practise using prepositions of place, in, on, and at, naturally and in real, meaningful context in this episode of Thinking in English Grammar! title: La aventura de la vida, parte 11
summary: They made it out of XibalbГЎ! They beat the odds, escaping the godsвҖҷ tricks and traps. They climbed the roots of the ceiba tree and found their way back to the land of the living. But this adventure isnвҖҷt over yet. This story is in the third person and present tense, with multiple other verb forms used as needed to tell the story. Important vocabulary in the story includes: вҖңagarraвҖқ (grab), вҖңgiraвҖқ (rotate), вҖңrugidoвҖқ (growl), and вҖңcenizasвҖқ (ash). No matter where you are in your language journey, stories will help you on your way. title: Let's Learn English! Topic: Buying and Selling! рҹ’·рҹӣҚпёҸрҹ’і
podcast_name: Learn English with Bob the Canadian
summary: In this English lesson, I will help you learn some verbs we use to talk about buying and selling. These verbs are perfect to use when talking about shopping, business purchases, and online orders. In this English lesson, you will learn these verbs: to buy, to sell, to purchase, to pay, to charge, to spend, to afford, to order, to refund, to return, to make an offer, to accept an offer, to negotiate, to discount, to resell, to trade, to invoice, and more! By the end of this free English class, you will be able to talk about prices, payments, orders, and simple business situations with more confidence and accuracy. I hope you enjoy this English lesson about buying and selling.

title: 208: Auguri tramezzino!
podcast_name: Easy Italian: Learn Italian with real conversations | Imparare l'italiano con conversazioni reali
summary: Ma prima ovviamente olimpiadi! Come ГЁ andato l'inizio? Trascrizione interattiva e Vocab Helper Support Easy Italian and get interactive transcripts, live vocabulary and bonus content: easyitalian.fm/membership Note dell'episodio https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-25XLf5S1f4&t=3s - Our Lunch Break in Slow Italian  Iniziamo con un piccolo aggiornamento sulle olimpiadi invernali!  Come sono iniziate? Come ГЁ andata la cerimonia di apertura a Milano e cosa dice la cittГ ? Alcuni si lamentano, altri sono felici, e altri non l'hanno guardata. Raffaele e Matteo? Scoperta archeologica del secolo! Hanno finalmente rinvenuto i resti della basilica di Vitruvio. Ma partiamo dall'inizio. Chi era costui? Vitruvio https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marco_Vitruvio_Pollione Basilica di Vitruvio https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basilica_di_Fano Il tramezzino ha compiuto 100 anni!  https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandwich Ma il sandwich ГЁ molto piu' antico e alcuni (Matteo) lo apprezzano anche di piГ№! Ma quali sono le differenze, se ci sono, e qual ГЁ la storia del tramezzino? Vi anticipiamo che il nome "tramezzino" ГЁ stato per la prima volta proposto da Gabriele d'Annunzio https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gabriele_D%27Annunzio. Trascrizione Raffaele: [0:23] Buongiorno Matteo! (Buongiorno, come va?) Potrebbe andare meglio, ma ci rilassiamo godendoci lo sport e il suo spettacolo. Matteo: [0:41] Come avevamo promesso, dobbiamo riportare le nostre... cosa sta succedendo in Italia a Milano riguardo il super sport di questo mese. Raffaele: [0:54] E sГ¬, le Olimpiadi di Milano-Cortina. Matteo, hai comprato la tuta? Matteo: [0:59] Ho comprato la tuta... no! PerГІ ci penserГІ, se trovo qualcosa in giro. PerchГ© in realtГ , poi ne parleremo anche piГ№ avanti in questa sezione quando vi diremo le nostre impressioni, ma girando per la cittГ , a meno che non si va proprio nel centro centro, non si sente questa Olimpiade. ... title: Our World in English: The battle for the cowboy way of life
summary: People in Montana, US worry that they will lose the land the makes the place special.  Nearly half of all the land in the American west is owned by the federal government. Some people want to change that and sell the land to build houses and businesses. But in Montana, a western state known for wide open spaces and cowboys, lots of people rely on the government land. They worry that their cowboy way of life is under threat. In this short documentary, we meet Brud Smith, who owns a farm in Montana, and hear about how the place is changing. Transcript and vocabulary: https://www.bbc.co.uk/learningenglish/english/features/our-world-in-english/260213 This programme is based on the documentary The real Yellowstone: A battle for the cowboy way of life from Assignment on the BBC World Service. The original programme was reported by Ellie House and produced by Josephine Casserly. Find more about Assignment here https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p002vsn0

title: Simplified Speech #240 вҖ“ Japanese music
summary: Join Andrew and Indiana for a conversation about their love for J-Pop. In this episode, the hosts discuss how they originally found this genre and why it has become so famous globally. They talk about the вҖңCity PopвҖқ trend, the unexpected ways they stumbled upon their favorite bands, and why listening to 90s Japanese music makes them feel nostalgic. Finally, they share a list of albums and songs to help you start listening, too. Press play to practice your English and find some great new music! The Best Way to Learn with This Episode: Culips members get an interactive transcript, helpful study guide, and ad-free audio for this episode. Take your English to the next level by becoming a Culips member. Become a Culips member now: Click here. Members can access the ad-free version here: Click here. Join our Discord community to connect with other learners and get more English practice. Click here to join. Here are the listening recommendations made by Andrew and Indiana: Spacy вҖ“ Tatsuro Yamashita: Click here Wet Land вҖ“ Hiroshi Yoshimura: Click here Sugar Babe вҖ“ Songs: Click here Happy End вҖ“ Kazamachi Roman: Click here Haruomi Hosono вҖ“ Hosono House: Click here PONPONPON вҖ“ Kyary Pamyu Pamyu: Click here Fun Fact Andrew mentions that listening to Japanese electronic music makes him nostalgic for old video games. This is no coincidence! Haruomi Hosono (recommended by both hosts in this episode) was a founder of Yellow Magic Orchestra, a band that pioneered using arcade sounds in pop music. Their 1978 hit вҖңComputer GameвҖқ actually sampled sounds from Space Invaders!

title: News in Slow Spanish - #883 - Best Spanish Program for Intermediate Learners
summary: Comenzaremos la primera parte del programa discutiendo la actualidad. El martes se publicГі el ГҚndice de PercepciГіn de la CorrupciГіn 2025. Discutiremos algunas de las tendencias que incluye el informe. A continuaciГіn, hablaremos del Гәltimo tratado de control de armas nucleares que quedaba entre Rusia y Estados Unidos, que expirГі el pasado jueves. Por primera vez en mГЎs de 50 aГұos, el mundo no tiene ningГәn tratado de control de armas nucleares. El segmento de ciencia lo dedicaremos a un estudio que argumenta que los alimentos ultraprocesados deberГӯan regularse de forma muy similar al tabaco. Y, para acabar, hablaremos de la Super Bowl, que tuvo lugar el pasado domingo. Es un gran acontecimiento deportivo y cultural que se celebra en EE. UU.  El resto del episodio de hoy lo dedicaremos a la lengua y la cultura espaГұolas. La primera conversaciГіn incluirГЎ ejemplos del tema de gramГЎtica de la semana, Adverbs: Overview. En esta conversaciГіn hablaremos de Barcelona, ciudad mediterrГЎnea poseedora de una arquitectura modernista maravillosa. 2026 va a ser un aГұo muy especial para esta ciudad: la UNESCO-UIA la ha nombrado Capital Mundial de la Arquitectura 2026. AdemГЎs, se celebra el centenario de la muerte del famoso arquitecto Antonio GaudГӯ. Y, en nuestra Гәltima conversaciГіn, aprenderemos a usar una nueva expresiГіn espaГұola, Malas lenguas. La usaremos para hablar del bisabuelo del rey Felipe VI, actual rey de EspaГұa. Es decir, Alfonso XIII, llamado el Africano. Se casГі joven con Victoria Eugenia de Battenberg, nieta de la reina Victoria de Inglaterra. Pero lo que parecГӯa un cuento de hadas, terminГі siendo un teatro. El matrimonio tuvo una relaciГіn distante, y la madre del rey y su nuera nunca congeniaron.   Los paГӯses occidentales caen a nuevos mГӯnimos en un Гӯndice global de corrupciГіn Por primera vez en mГЎs de 50 aГұos, el mundo no tiene ningГәn tratado de control de armas nucleares Un estudio compara los riesgos de salud asociados a los alimentos ultraprocesados con los de los cigarrillos Un espectГЎculo histГіrico del descanso de la Super Bowl eclipsa al propio partido Barcelona 2026, Capital Mundial de la Arquitectura Alfonso XIII, el Africano

title: News in Slow German - #501 - Easy German Radio
summary: Wir beginnen den ersten Teil unseres Programms mit einer Diskussion Гјber einige aktuelle Ereignisse. Am Dienstag wurde der Korruptionswahrnehmungsindex fГјr 2025 verГ¶ffentlicht. Wir werden einige Trends des Berichts diskutieren. Danach sprechen wir Гјber den letzten noch bestehenden Atomwaffenkontrollvertrag zwischen Russland und den USA, der am vergangenen Donnerstag ausgelaufen ist. Zum ersten Mal seit Гјber 50 Jahren gibt es nun weltweit keine AtomwaffenkontrollvertrГӨge mehr. In unserem Wissenschaftsthema behandeln wir heute eine neue Studie, die sich dafГјr ausspricht, hochverarbeitete Lebensmittel ГӨhnlich wie Tabak zu regulieren. Und zum Schluss sprechen wir Гјber den Super Bowl, der am vergangenen Sonntag stattgefunden hat. Dieses Sportereignis hat in den USA auch kulturell eine groГҹe Bedeutung.  Der Rest des Programms ist der deutschen Sprache und Kultur gewidmet. Das Thema der heutigen Grammatiklektion ist: Der Infinitivsatz вҖ“ Verbs with вҖһzu": Part 2. Die deutschen Brauereien stecken in einer Krise. Viele von ihnen setzen lГӨngst nicht mehr nur auf ihr Haupterzeugnis, Bier. Eine norddeutsche Brauerei will nun vegane Fleischersatzprodukte aus Brauresten herstellen. Ende Januar fand wГӨhrend der Messe Jagd und Hund in Dortmund die Deutsche Meisterschaft im Hirschrufen statt. Teilnehmer aus sechs BundeslГӨndern versuchten, Hirsche nach Strich und Faden zu imitieren. Genau das ist auch die Redewendung dieser Woche: Nach Strich und Faden. Am Ende konnte Thomas Soltwedel aus Mecklenburg-Vorpommern den Wettbewerb fГјr sich entscheiden.   Zunahme von Korruption auch in westlichen LГӨndern Zum ersten Mal seit Гјber 50 Jahren gibt es weltweit keinen Atomwaffenvertrag mehr Neue Studie vergleicht Gesundheitsrisiken von hochverarbeiteten Lebensmitteln mit denen von Zigaretten Historische Super-Bowl-Halbzeitshow stellt den Football in den Schatten WГјrstchen aus Bier Die Deutsche Meisterschaft im Hirschrufen

title: Adjectives you need to describe change in English [574]
summary: рҹҡЁ If you use English at work, these phrases matter. В These are the only English phrases I allow my private students to use at work, and the ones I tell them to stop using.В  рҹ‘ү Download the PDF (вӮ¬9): https://www.englishlessonviaskype.com/the-english-phrases-i-would-let-my-own-students-use-at-work/ In this episode, you will learn natural English words used to describe change, from small adjustments to major transformations. Many learners know basic words like big or small, but they are not always sure how to describe change clearly and accurately. This can make your English sound simple or repetitive. In this lesson, I explain useful adjectives like dramatic, subtle, radical, minimal, and profound. You will learn what they really mean, when to use them, and how they sound in real conversations. By the end of this episode, you will be able to: вҖў describe changes more precisely В вҖў choose the right word for formal and everyday situations В вҖў sound more natural and confident when talking about decisions, events, and life changes If you want your English to sound clearer, more natural, and more confident, this episode will help. Share Your Thoughts My Business English Phrase Guide shows you exactly what to say in meetings, emails, and when problems happen - and which common phrases make you sound unprofessional. title: Let's Talk about Hair! (with Julia)
summary: In this cozy winter episode, Shana is snowed in with her daughters on the East Coast after a big snowstorm. While it looks like a magical winter wonderland outside, things feel a little chaotic inside the house. Then Julia, who's seven, decides she wants to be a guest on the podcast. title: #363 The Hidden Differences in How We Think
summary: I recently learnt that not everyone thinks the same way I do. In fact, there are some areas in which our brains differ greatly. For me, this is a fascinating topic. How do I know the green colour you see, is the same as the green colour I see? In this episode, I share a few different ways in which our brains are different, and what this might mean for us living our daily lives, and the implications as language learners.  So if you want to learn more about your brain and how it works, listen to this episode, and don't forget to share your own experiences in the comments. title: 374. Is There an AI Bubble? (English Vocabulary Lesson)
summary: Artificial intelligence is everywhere right now. We use it to write emails, generate images, recommend videos, and even help study English. Some people believe AI will completely change the world, just like the internet did. Others are more sceptical and worry that the excitement has gone too far. Every day on the news, we hear about new AI startups worth billions of dollars, governments investing huge amounts of money, and tech companies racing to become the next AI leader. There is a lot of optimism, but also a lot of hype. Some people are starting to question whether we are we seeing real, long-term growth, or are we living through an AI bubble? In todayвҖҷs episode of Thinking in English, weвҖҷll explore what an economic bubble really is, look at famous bubbles from history, and ask whether artificial intelligence is following the same pattern or whether this time is different. At the same time, weвҖҷll learn some useful English vocabulary! summary: In this fun and practical English lesson, youвҖҷll learn quirky, everyday idioms and expressions that native speakers use all the time in real conversations. These sayings can sound strange at first, but once you understand them, they make your English sound more natural, relaxed, and confident. In the English class I'll help you learn phrases like: an apple a day keeps the doctor away, to hold your horses, to spill the beans, to hit the nail on the head, to bark up the wrong tree, to pull someoneвҖҷs leg, to go off the rails, to be on thin ice, to throw in the towel, and many more! YouвҖҷll hear what each one means, when people use it, and how it to pronounce each one. By the end of this lesson, youвҖҷll be able to recognize these playful expressions when you hear them and start using them naturally in your own speaking. I hope you enjoy this English lesson about idioms!

title: 207: Una ricetta olimpica!
podcast_name: Easy Italian: Learn Italian with real conversations | Imparare l'italiano con conversazioni reali
summary: Cucinare! Ma non solo, mettetevi comodi, ma non troppo, perchГ© dopo qualche chiacchiera poi dobbiamo preparare la ricetta. Trascrizione interattiva e Vocab Helper Support Easy Italian and get interactive transcripts, live vocabulary and bonus content: easyitalian.fm/membership Note dell'episodio https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fhn3v844_Ts&t=1s -  Italians Using the CONGIUNTIVO (real-life examples) Iniziano le olimpiadi invernali Milano Cortina 2026! E noi? Le osserviamo e vi riportiamo un po' il "dietro le quinte" o almeno vi raccontiamo il lato della cittГ  di Milano che viviamo tutti i giorni. Partiamo con: come ГЁ iniziato tutto, come ГЁ l'organizzazione delle olimpiadi? Siamo alle solite? Poca organizzazione e tante lamentele? A quanto pare un po' si. Raffaele ci racconta come sta andando e che notizie ci sono. CuriositГ  per la fiaccola che ГЁ arrivata a Milano, molti "tedofori" https://it.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tedoforo Sono state personaggi famosi italiani ma non sportivi famosi. Non proprio quello che ci si aspetta da coloro che portano in giro la fiaccola delle olimpiadi. Passiamo dalle olimpiadi agli affreschi. Ma non affreschi qualsiasi! Parliamo di affreschi che assomigliano un po' troppo a politici italiani. Ma chi? A quanto pare un angelo assomiglia un po' troppo al presidente dei ministri italiano! Concludiamo in cucina! La ricetta della pasta al forno che Matteo ha fatto nell'ultimo festival organizzato dalla scuola di italiano Joy of languages https://joyoflanguages.com/  Ingredienti: Pasta corta 400 g / 14 once Short pasta 400 g / 14 oz Passata 1l / 4 cups Tomato purГ©e 1 L / 4 cups Una melanzana grande o due piccole One large eggplant or two small ones Scamorza affumicata (o mozzarella) 200g / 7 once Smoked scamorza cheese (or mozzarella) 200 g / 7 oz Parmigiano 50 g / 1,7 once Parmigiano cheese 50 g / 1.7 oz Pangrattato 50 g / 1,7 once Breadcrumbs 50 g / 1.7 oz Uno spicchio di aglio One clove of garlic Sale Salt Olio Oil Strumenti: Pentola Pot Pentolino per il sugo Small saucepan for the sauce Teglia 25 cm x 17 cm Medium baking dish (about 10 Г— 7 inches), suitable for 4 servings Coltello Knife Tagliere Cutting board Ciotola Bowl Scolapasta Colander Cucchiaio di legno Wooden spoon Mestolo per sugo Ladle for sauce Padella Pan / Skille Ricetta:  In una padella, scaldare olio e uno spicchio dвҖҷaglio schiacciato con il coltello; quando fa le bollicine, aggiungere il sugo.  In a pan, heat the oil and one clove of garlic crushed with a knife; when it starts bubbling, add the sauce. Tagliare le melanzane e togliere un poвҖҷ di polpa.  Cut the eggplants and remove some of the flesh. Cuocere le melanzane in padella con il sale e aggiungere acqua o vino se si asciugano troppo.  Cook the eggplants in the pan with salt and add water or wine if they dry out too much. Abbassare la fiamma quando le melanzane si rimpiccioliscono.  Lower the heat when the eggplants shrink. Far bollire lвҖҷacqua, aggiungere il sale e cuocere la pasta.  Bring the water to a boil, add salt, and cook the pasta. Accendere il forno a 180В° / Gas mark 4 / 356В°F.  Preheat the oven to 180В°C / Gas mark 4 / 356В°F. Tagliare la scamorza: metГ  finemente e metГ  a fette per la parte sopra.  Cut the scamorza: half finely and half into slices for the top. Scolare la pasta al dente e mescolarla in una ciotola con sugo e melanzane.  Drain the pasta al dente and mix it in a bowl with the sauce and eggplants. Mettere in teglia uno strato di pasta e aggiungere la scamorza tagliata finemente.  Place a layer of pasta in the baking dish and add the finely chopped scamorza. Sopra mettere scamorza, pangrattato e parmigiano.  On top, add scamorza, breadcrumbs, and Parmigiano. Cuocere per 10 minuti; usare il grill se serve dorare la superficie.  Bake for 10 minutes; use the grill/broiler if needed to brown the top. Mmmm... che fame рҹҳӢ Trascrizione Raffaele: [0:09] Sei pronto? Sei pronto? Hai comprato la tuta? Matteo: [0:26] Siamo pronti? No, perГІ sai cosa? Ci stavo pensando. Adesso non ГЁ che sono proprio un tipo da tuta. PerГІ ne sto vedendo cosГ¬ tante in giro per Milano di tute delle Olimpiadi, che quasi quasi... forse anche solo la parte di sopra: ГЁ carina. Raffaele: [0:57] Ma si vendono separate? ... title: #83 Michelin : les pneus, le guide, et Clermont-Ferrand
summary: рҹӣһ Michelin. Tu connais sГ»rement ce nom. Mais pourquoi exactement ? Les pneus ? Le guide ? Les restaurants Г©toilГ©s ? Dans cet Г©pisode, on te raconte la grande histoire de Michelin, une aventure familiale nГ©e au 19бөү siГЁcle dans une petite ville du centre de la France : Clermont-Ferrand. On revient sur les dГ©buts de lвҖҷentreprise, son rГҙle dans lвҖҷhistoire des transports (vГ©lo, automobile, train, et mГӘme avion), et sur ses nombreuses innovations.  On explique aussi le coup de gГ©nie marketing de Michelin : distribuer gratuitement un guide pour faire rouler les gensвҖҰ et vendre plus de pneus.  Et on te raconte comment sont nГ©es les fameuses Г©toiles Michelin, dans un petit atelier, presque par hasard. On te partage aussi nos recommandations de restaurants Г©toilГ©s : un Г  lвҖҷouest de la France, un Г  lвҖҷestвҖҰ et un Г  Paris. вӯҗ Dans la deuxiГЁme partie, on fait un zoom sur Clermont-Ferrand, le berceau de Michelin et toujours son siГЁge mondial aujourd'hui. Comment lвҖҷentreprise a faГ§onnГ© la ville, crГ©Г© des quartiers entiers pour ses employГ©s, et marquГ© durablement la vie locale. рҹҺ¬ Et pour finir, Nathan te recommande un film culte que tous les FranГ§ais ont vu au moins une fois : LвҖҷAile ou la Cuisse. Une comГ©die incontournable sur la France des annГ©es 1970, la gastronomieвҖҰ et la malbouffe рҹҚҪпёҸ La transcription dГ©taillГ©e de l'Г©pisode est disponible ici.  Merci d'ГӘtre lГ  et de soutenir le podcast. title: News In Slow Italian #682- Italian News, Grammar, and Expressions
summary: Apriamo la prima parte della puntata con l'attualitГ . Si sta diffondendo una crescente ondata di indignazione contro le forze dell'Agenzia statunitense per l'immigrazione e le dogane (Immigration and Customs Enforcement statunitense), note come ICE. Le proteste ormai vanno ben oltre i confini degli Stati Uniti e si sono estese a livello globale. La nostra seconda notizia riguarda l'Europa che si sta muovendo verso l'introduzione di divieti sull'uso dei social media per i minori. In Spagna, ad esempio, ГЁ previsto il divieto di accesso ai social per i ragazzi sotto i 16 anni. Misure simili sono allo studio in diversi Paesi europei. Quindi, parleremo di un cambiamento importante in casa Tesla. Il suo amministratore delegato, Elon Musk, ha deciso di invertire la rotta, spostando l'attenzione dalle auto alla produzione di robot su larga scala. E infine, parleremo di una tendenza piuttosto triste. I ristoranti tradizionali francesi stanno chiudendo a un ritmo allarmante, circa 25 al giorno in tutta la Francia. Il declino ГЁ dovuto all'aumento dei costi, al calo dei profitti e al cambiamento delle abitudini alimentari dei consumatori.В   В   La seconda parte della puntata ГЁ dedicata alla lingua e alla cultura italiana. L'argomento grammaticale di oggi ГЁ Exclamative adverbs. Ne troverete diversi esempi nel dialogo dedicato al celebre stilista Valentino, scomparso di recente, che ha contribuito a diffondere in maniera significativa lo stile e il gusto italiano nel mondo. Nel finale ci soffermeremo sull'espressione idiomatica di oggi: Dare il la. La incontrerete nel dialogo dedicato a un ritrovamento archeologico di grande rilievo a Fano, nelle Marche: resti che gli studiosi attribuiscono alla basilica progettata da Vitruvio, l'architetto, ingegnere e teorico romano che ha influenzato secoli di cultura occidentale.  - Cresce l'indignazione internazionale contro le pratiche dell'ICE  - L'Europa sempre piГ№ compatta verso il divieto dei social media per i minori  - Musk inverte la rotta di Tesla, abbandonando le auto per concentrarsi sulla produzione in serie di robot  - I bistrot francesi stanno scomparendo a un ritmo allarmante  - Valentino e la fine di un'epoca della moda italiana  - La basilica che cercavano da 500 anni

title: News in Slow German - #500 - Study German While Listening to the News
summary: Wir beginnen den ersten Teil unseres Programms mit einer Diskussion Гјber einige aktuelle Ereignisse. Die EmpГ¶rung Гјber die US-amerikanische Einwanderungs- und ZollbehГ¶rde ICE wГӨchst. Die Proteste gehen weit Гјber die USA hinaus und haben sich inzwischen auf die ganze Welt ausgeweitet. Danach sprechen wir Гјber PlГӨne in Europa, Social-Media-Verbote fГјr Kinder einzufГјhren. Spanien will Kindern unter 16 Jahren den Zugang zu Social Media verbieten. Г„hnliche MaГҹnahmen sind auch in anderen europГӨischen LГӨndern im GesprГӨch. AnschlieГҹend sprechen wir Гјber einen Kurswechsel bei Tesla. CEO Elon Musk will Tesla strategisch neu ausrichten вҖ“ weg von der Automobilproduktion und hin zur Massenproduktion von Robotern. Und zum Schluss sprechen wir Гјber einen Trend in Frankreich, der mich persГ¶nlich sehr traurig macht. Dort verschwinden traditionelle franzГ¶sische Restaurants mit einer alarmierenden Geschwindigkeit. Es sind etwa 25 pro Tag! Dies ist auf steigende Kosten, sinkende Gewinne und verГӨnderte Essgewohnheiten zurГјckzufГјhren.  Der Rest des Programms ist der deutschen Sprache und Kultur gewidmet. Das Thema der heutigen Grammatiklektion ist: Der Infinitivsatz вҖ“ Verbs with вҖһzu": Part 1. Wer seine angemietete Wohnung untervermietet, darf damit keinen Gewinn machen. Das ging aus einem Urteil des Bundesgerichtshofs hervor. In diesem konkreten Fall hatte jemand eine mehr als doppelt so hohe Miete vom Untermieter verlangt, als im Hauptmietvertrag festgelegt war. Rechtfertigen wollte er dies mit dem Argument, dass die Wohnung mГ¶bliert untervermietet wurde. Unsere Redewendung diese Woche ist Sich in Schale schmeiГҹen. Dazu passt nichts besser als der berГјhmt-berГјchtigte Wiener Opernball, der eine der strengsten Kleiderordnungen der Welt hat. Er findet dieses Jahr am 12. Februar statt.   Wachsende internationale EmpГ¶rung Гјber die Vorgehensweise von ICE Europa nГӨhert sich einem Social-Media-Verbot fГјr Kinder Kurswechsel bei Tesla: Musk will kГјnftig Roboter bauen Frankreichs Bistro-Kultur droht zu verschwinden Kein Gewinn durch Untervermietung Der Wiener Opernball
