title: Simplified Speech #236 вҖ“ Previewing the Winter Olympics
summary: In this Culips episode, Andrew and his friend Luke talk about the Winter Olympics and why they are such a big deal in Canada. They chat about Olympic ice hockey, the return of pro NHL players, and the excitement and controversy around the next Games in Italy. You also hear them share their favorite winter sports, talk about strange Olympic events, and play a fun memory game where they try to name past Winter Olympic host cities. What youвҖҷll learn with this episode: How to understand and use common conversation words like вҖңchatter,вҖқ вҖңlineup,вҖқ вҖңcontroversy,вҖқ and вҖңrivalryвҖқ How to follow a natural back-and-forth discussion about sports, history, and culture How English speakers react in the moment with expressions like вҖңholy molyвҖқ and вҖңitвҖҷs escaping meвҖқ How to give opinions and make predictions in English This episode is perfect for you if: You want listening practice with real, casual English conversation You want to build your speaking skills for everyday topics like sports, travel, and culture You want to learn useful vocabulary that helps you sound more natural and confident in English conversations The Best Way to Learn with This Episode: Culips members get an interactive transcript, helpful study guide, and ad-free audio for this episode. Take your English to the next level by becoming a Culips member. Become a Culips member now: Click here. Members can access the ad-free version: Click here. Join our Discord community to connect with other learners and get more English practice. Click here to join.В  Fact check:  Ice Dancing vs. Figure Skating Jumps The Claim: Luke mentions knowing the names of maneuvers like the вҖңtriple LutzвҖқ and вҖңtriple SalchowвҖқ because his mom watched Ice Dancing. The Fact: Those are Figure Skating jumps. In competitive Ice Dancing, athletes are actually forbidden from performing these types of multi-rotation jumps. It is a common mix-up, but the two are separate disciplines!  Surfing as a вҖңOne-OffвҖқ Showcase The Claim: Andrew thought Surfing was a one-time showcase sport for the Summer Olympics, similar to breakdancing. The Fact: Unlike breakdancing (which was only for Paris 2024), Surfing is a permanent Olympic sport. It debuted in Tokyo 2020, appeared in Paris 2024, and is already confirmed for the Los Angeles 2028 Games.  The History of Ski Jumping The Claim: Luke suggested that ski jumping started at the Calgary 1988 Olympics with Eddie the Eagle. The Fact: Ski jumping is actually one of the original Winter Olympic sports. It has been in every Winter Games since the very first one in 1924 (Chamonix, France)вҖ”64 years before the Calgary Games.  When the Games вҖңStaggeredвҖқ (The 2-Year Gap) The Claim: Andrew and Luke discussed the Games being вҖңstaggeredвҖқ (Summer and Winter in different years) starting after 1988. The Fact: The change actually happened after 1992. Both the Summer and Winter Games were held in 1992 (Albertville and Barcelona). The first time the Winter Olympics were held in their own separate year was Lillehammer 1994.  CanadaвҖҷs 2010 Gold Medal Record The Claim: Luke estimated that Canada won about 10 gold medals in Vancouver 2010. The Fact: Canada actually won 14 gold medals in 2010. At the time, this set a world record for the most gold medals ever won by a single country at any Winter Olympics.

title: News In Slow French #773 - Intermediate French Weekly Program
summary: Nous commencerons notre section sur l'actualitГ© en abordant un Г©vГЁnement tragique qui a eu lieu Г  Sydney, en Australie. Le premier jour de la fГӘte de Hanoukka, quinze personnes ont Г©tГ© tuГ©es dans une fusillade Г  Bondi Beach. Ensuite, nous parlerons du quatriГЁme anniversaire de l'invasion totale de l'Ukraine par la Russie qui approche et nous verrons ce que cela signifie pour l'Europe. Notre section scientifique sera consacrГ©e Г  une Г©tude selon laquelle la monogamie est beaucoup plus courante chez les humains que chez les primates. Et nous conclurons la premiГЁre partie de l'Г©mission d'aujourd'hui par une conversation sur le choix du magazine TIME de dГ©signer les architectes de l'IA personnalitГ©s de l'annГ©e 2025.В   В   Le reste de l'Г©mission d'aujourd'hui sera consacrГ© Г  la langue et Г  la culture franГ§aises. Notre point de grammaire de la semaine sera : The Plural of Adjectives: Adjectives ending in -al, -eau, -s and -x (add summary). Nous parlerons d'une maladie qui inquiГЁte beaucoup les agriculteurs franГ§ais : la dermatose nodulaire. En raison de l'augmentation des cas de contamination, de nombreuses bГӘtes risquent d'ГӘtre abattues. Nous terminerons avec l'expression de la semaine : Croire dur comme fer. Nous discuterons de l'incroyable succГЁs en France comme Г  l'Г©tranger de la publicitГ© de NoГ«l d'IntermarchГ©, qui met en scГЁne un loup qui se met Г  manger des lГ©gumes.  - La tolГ©rance envers la violence symbolique antisГ©mite encourage la violence rГ©elle  - L'invasion totale de l'Ukraine par la Russie entre bientГҙt dans sa cinquiГЁme annГ©e  - Selon une Г©tude, la monogamie est bien plus courante chez les humains que chez les primates  - Le magazine TIME dГ©signe les В« architectes de l'IA В» personnalitГ©s de l'annГ©e 2025  - Le gouvernement franГ§ais prend des mesures radicales face Г  la dermatose  - La pub de NoГ«l d'IntermarchГ© cartonne dans le monde entier

title: News In Slow Italian #675- Intermediate Italian Weekly Program
summary: Apriamo la nostra rassegna di attualitГ  con un evento tragico avvenuto a Sydney, in Australia. Quindici persone sono state uccise in una sparatoria di massa a Bondi Beach durante il primo giorno delle celebrazioni di Hanukkah. L'argomento successivo riguarda l'avvicinarsi del quarto anniversario dell'invasione russa su larga scala dell'Ucraina e ciГІ che questo significa per l'Europa. La sezione scientifica ГЁ dedicata a uno studio che mostra come gli esseri umani siano significativamente piГ№ monogami rispetto agli altri primati. E concludiamo la prima parte della puntata con una conversazione sulla scelta di TIME di nominare gli "Architetti di IA" Personaggi dell'Anno 2025.В   В   La seconda parte della puntata ГЁ dedicata alla lingua e alla cultura italiana. L'argomento grammaticale di oggi ГЁ Derived Adverbs. Ne troverete diversi esempi nel dialogo che segue, dedicato a un'insolita protesta nella cittГ  di Como, dove i cittadini si oppongono alla decisione del Comune di abbattere numerosi alberi di ciliegio. Nel finale ci soffermeremo sull'espressione idiomatica di oggi: Essere/sentirsi un pesce fuor d'acqua. Il dialogo in cui la utilizzeremo prende spunto da una figura iconica della storia del tennis italiano e volto televisivo molto noto: Nicola Pietrangeli, scomparso di recente all'etГ  di 92 anni.  - La tolleranza verso la violenza simbolica antisemita favorisce la violenza reale  - L'invasione russa su larga scala dell'Ucraina si avvicina al quinto anno  - Uno studio rileva che gli esseri umani sono significativamente piГ№ monogami degli altri primati  - Il TIME nomina gli "Architetti dell'IA" Personaggi dell'anno 2025  - La battaglia dei ciliegi di Como  - Гү morto Nicola Pietrangeli

title: News in Slow Spanish - #875 - Study Spanish While Listening to the News
summary: Comenzaremos nuestra discusiГіn de la actualidad con la tragedia ocurrida en SГӯdney, Australia. 15 personas fueron asesinadas durante un ataque con armas de fuego en la playa de Bondi, durante el primer dГӯa de la festividad de JanucГЎ. Nuestro siguiente tema serГЎ el cuarto aniversario de la invasiГіn rusa a gran escala de Ucrania, y lo que significa para Europa. El segmento de ciencia lo dedicaremos a un estudio que cataloga a los humanos como considerablemente mГЎs monГіgamos que otros primates. Y concluiremos la primera parte del programa de hoy hablando sobre la decisiГіn de la revista TIME de nombrar a los "Arquitectos de la IA" como Persona del AГұo 2025.  El resto del episodio de hoy lo dedicaremos a la lengua y la cultura espaГұolas. La primera conversaciГіn incluirГЎ ejemplos del tema de gramГЎtica de la semana, Present Perfect vs. Preterite. En esta conversaciГіn hablaremos de poesГӯa mГӯstica. Recordaremos la poesГӯa del Renacimiento de Santa Teresa de JesГәs, y las canciones del ГЎlbum LUX de la cantante espaГұola Rosalia. ВҝEs posible que encontremos semejanzasвҖҰ? Y, en nuestra Гәltima conversaciГіn, aprenderemos a usar una nueva expresiГіn espaГұola,Tener algo en la punta de la lengua. La usaremos para hablar de deporte. Pero con un matiz muy especial: el deporte de exhibiciГіn de las fiestas tradicionales de los pueblos espaГұoles. Estos eventos suelen mezclar competiciones folclГіricas con el ambiente festivo local, y forman parte de la identidad cultural de nuestras comunidades. ВЎUn patrimonio cultural que no podemos echar a perder!   Tolerar la violencia antisemita simbГіlica promueve la verdadera violencia La invasiГіn rusa a gran escala de Ucrania se aproxima a su cuarto aГұo Un estudio indica que los humanos somos considerablemente mГЎs monГіgamos que otros primates La revista TIME nombra a los "Arquitectos de la IA" Persona del AГұo 2025 PoesГӯa mГӯstica espaГұola Tener algo en la punta de la lengua

title: 209: La flor del ceibo
podcast_name: ВЎCuГ©ntame! | Learn Spanish with Comprehensible Input
summary: In this episode of ВЎCuГ©ntame!, listeners travel to Argentina as the podcast continues its series of legends from the Spanish-speaking world. Marta tells the beautiful and inspiring legend of the ceibo flower, a powerful national symbol of Argentina. The story takes place before the conquest of the Americas and centers on AnahГӯ, a young Indigenous woman who loved her land, her people, and her community. Through her courage, voice, and resilience, the legend explains why the bright red flower of the ceibo tree became a symbol of love for the land, strength, and remembrance. Transcript here! title: Irregular Verbs with Bonnie and Clyde
summary: Who doesnвҖҷt love a good crime story from the 1930s?  Add romance, fast cars, and a nation in chaosвҖҰ and youвҖҷve got Bonnie and Clyde. In this episode, I share aВ simplified, learner-friendly versionВ of their true storyвҖ”set during theВ Great DepressionвҖ”and pack it withВ irregular verbs.  YouвҖҷll hear how newspapers turned two criminals into legends, why the public sympathized with them, and how their story became one of the most famous love-and-crime tales in U.S. history. YouвҖҷll Learn:   The meaning ofВ infamous   Why crime increased during theВ 1930s   How newspapers turned Bonnie and Clyde into legends   10 essentialВ irregular verbsВ in context (+ TONS of others we already learned in other irregular verb episodes) Focus Verbs: steal (stole)В В·В meet (met)В В·В have (had)В В·В drive (drove)В В·В shoot (shot)В В·hurt (hurt)В В·В catch (caught)В В·В give (gave)В В·В hide (hid)В В·В forget (forgot) Mentioned in the Episode   Get theВ full transcript, quizzes, worksheets, and videosВ inside  вҒ The Academy Learn more about your ad choices. title: Our world in English: Love in a warzone
summary: How far would you go for love? When Czech writer Adela falls in love with artist Konstantin Zhorkin, she moves to Kharkiv, in Ukraine, to be with him. Kharkiv is just 30 kilometres from the Russian border and has been hit by some of the worst attacks of the war in Ukraine. In this short documentary, Adela tells her personal story of what life is like in Kharkiv as the war continues.  Based on the BBC Eye documentary, Kharkiv: Love in a War Zone, first broadcast on 25th September 2025. The orginal programme was reported by Albina Kovalyova and produced by Louise Hidalgo.  Find the full programme here: https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/w3ct82ky More from BBC Eye: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLz_B0PFGIn4fa8LK1lptsvoA_spfJhzda  (Photo: Adela and Konstantin. title: Do We Live in a Post-Truth Society? (English Vocabulary Lesson)
summary: In a world of social media, fake news, and deepfakesвҖҰ how can we know whatвҖҷs true anymore? The phrase вҖңpost-truthвҖқ has become one of the defining ideas of our time. Politicians lie without consequence, misinformation spreads faster than facts, and many people seem to trust their feelings more than solid evidence. As a result, truth itself seems less influential than emotion or personal belief. In todayвҖҷs episode, weвҖҷre going to explore what it means to live in a post-truth societyвҖҰ. a society where the line between fact and opinion is increasingly blurred, and where it can feel harder than ever to know what is real. At the same time, IвҖҷll introduce some new and useful vocabulary, and we will practice our English listening comprehension! title: Let's Learn English! Topic: Annoying Situations! рҹҡҰрҹ•ҜпёҸвҸ°
podcast_name: Learn English with Bob the Canadian
summary: In this English lesson, youвҖҷll learn common phrases that English speakers use to talk about everyday annoying situations. These are the little problems that can mess up your schedule, slow things down, or quietly push your patience to its limit. Learning these expressions will help you explain real-life frustrations in a clear, natural way. In this class, weвҖҷll cover phrases like: locked out, running late for work, stuck in traffic, and oversleeping. YouвҖҷll also learn how to talk about things such as a power outage, missing the bus, spilling coffee, losing your keys, a dead phone battery, forgetting your wallet, and other small but irritating moments. By the end of the lesson, youвҖҷll be ready to describe lifeвҖҷs everyday annoyances more confidently and sound more natural in English conversations. I hope you enjoy this useful English lesson focused on common frustrating situations. Note: This is the audio portion of a Youtube English lesson which you can watch right here and the full transcript is in the description there:вҒ  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6czx_HsbUFo or by searching Youtube for, "Bob the Canadian Annoying Things"

title: Giving Effectively (with James Rayton)
summary: Stephen talks to James Rayton of the organizationВ Giving What We Can. Stephen and James talk about what effective altruism is, how it is possible to research and calculate effective giving, the possible problems and limits of effective altruism, how giving is affected by our emotions, how much it costs to save a human life, the cuts in aid across the world in 2025, how much difference we can all make, how and why SEND7 have been giving 10% of its profit to charity, and about whether it is right to talk about money and charity like this, or whether donating to charity is something which is supposed to be done in private and not spoken about. Do you have a question? Stephen will answer it next week. Send a voice note at www.speakpipe.com/send7 Or an email to podcast@send7.org Get access to all of our transcripts, vocabulary lists, worksheets, and world news quizzes by becoming a supporter at send7.org . title: 199: Il mercatino di Natale... FANTASMA!
podcast_name: Easy Italian: Learn Italian with real conversations | Imparare l'italiano con conversazioni reali
summary: Mercatini di Natale! Ormai ci siamo, e c'ГЁ qualcuno che ha gia fatto un bel giro per i mercatini. Niente paura, subito dopo i mercatini ci aspetta una perfetta pausa, ovviamente per mangiare, le pizze piu' buone del mondo! Trascrizione interattiva e Vocab Helper Support Easy Italian and get interactive transcripts, live vocabulary and bonus content: easyitalian.fm/membership Note dell'episodio Dreaming, Hoping: Use the Italian вҖҳSeвҖҷ Like a Native Speaker - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5LA0NEMt6OM Natale! I mercatini!  Aiuto!  Ma prima di scappare, Raffaele con le ultime forze rimaste dopo un finesettimana dedicato ai mercatini di Natale, ci racconta i mercatini in Italia, i piu' interessanti e i piu' particolari. Uno tra tutti? Quello fantasma... ad Apice. Durante la prima sezione, Raffaele un verbo molto particolare: SMAMMARE. Ma cosa vuol dire? In origine si riferiva al "sottrarsi alla tutela materna", ha poi assunto il significato di "svignarsela, andarsene via, levarsi di torno. E' un termine molto usato al sud. Parliamo poi di... CIBO! O meglio, parliamo di pizza. Quella migliore del mondo. Ma con grande sorpresa di Raffaele, alcune delle pizzerie nella parte alta della classifica non sono campane (della regione Campania in Italia) ma non sono nemmeno italiane, sono in giro per il mondo, tra il Brasile e il Giappone! E voi? Avete mangiato una pizza molto molto buona ultimamente? Raccontateci tutto! Venite a trovarci sul nostro sito e lasciateci un messaggio! https://www.easyitalian.fm/ Trascrizione Raffaele: [0:09] E basta! Matteo: [0:23] Buongiorno! Raffaele: [0:24] Buongiorno a te, Matteo, come va? Matteo: [0:27] Bene, tu? Ti sento un po' annoiato? (Aggrinch-ato.) Aggrinch-ato, esiste? Raffaele: [0:36] Oggi faccio la parte del Grinch: basta con questi mercatini di Natale, basta con questi festeggiamenti prima del tempo, basta. Matteo: [0:50] La vita in Italia con i mercatini di Natale: com'ГЁ questa vita in Italia con i mercatini di Natale? ... What youвҖҷll learn with this episode: How to talk about cleaners, beauty products, and everyday items in natural English Useful words and expressions related to safety, health, ingredients, and household routines How native speakers share personal stories, give examples, and explain their lifestyle choices Simple ways people try to avoid strong smells, heavy-duty cleaners, and unclear ingredients This episode is perfect for you if: You are interested in learning how to avoid unsafe chemicals in the daily products that you use You want to talk about everyday health and safety in clear, natural English You want to learn useful words for cleaners, beauty products, and daily routines The Best Way to Learn with This Episode: Culips members get an interactive transcript, helpful study guide, and ad-free audio for this episode. title: #79 Notre relation complexe aux villages
summary: рҹҢҫрҹҗ„ Dans cet Г©pisode, on discute dвҖҷun sujet qui revient tout le temps autour de nous : oГ№ est-ce qu'on vit le mieux quand on a la trentaine ? En ville ou dans un village ? On partage nos expГ©riences personnelles, les endroits oГ№ on a vГ©cu, nos envies, nos contradictions. Et mГӘme nos peurs. On parle de ces villages qui deviennent trop touristiques, de ceux qui se vident parce que tout ferme. Et de ce quвҖҷon adore quand on вҖңse met au vertвҖқ Г  la campagne. ГҖ lвҖҷinverse, on raconte aussi toutes les raisons pour lesquelles la ville nous attire encore aujourdвҖҷhui. On nвҖҷest pas toujours dвҖҷaccord, et cвҖҷest ce qui rend lвҖҷГ©pisode vivant. title: News In Slow French #772 - Intermediate French Weekly Program
summary: Nous commencerons notre partieВ consacrГ©e Г В l'actualitГ© par une discussion sur un nouveau document publiГ© la semaine derniГЁre sur la stratГ©gie de sГ©curitГ© nationale amГ©ricaine.В Nous abordonsВ les Г©lГ©ments qui concernent directement l'Europe, ainsi que la prГ©diction d'un В«В effacementВ В» de la civilisation europГ©enne. Puis, nous commenteronsВ le classement annuelВ des 36 pays les plus riches, basГ© sur l'inflation,В la croissance du PIB,В l'emploiВ etВ les performances boursiГЁres. Notre section scientifique sera consacrГ©e Г  une Г©tude qui a identifiГ© cinqВ grandes phasesВ du dГ©veloppement duВ cerveau humain. Selon les chercheurs, il y aurait quatreВ pГ©riodes charniГЁresВ Г  9, 32, 66 et 83 ans, oГ№ des changements importantsВ ont lieuВ dans l'organisation neuronale. EtВ notre dernier sujet d'actualitГ©В concerne Pantone, quiВ a dГ©voilГ©В sa couleur de l'annГ©e 2026. Le choix de la couleur de l'annГ©e peut-il faire dГ©bat ?  В   Le reste de l'Г©mission d'aujourd'hui sera consacrГ© Г  la langue et Г  la culture franГ§aises. Notre point de grammaire de la semaine sera :В Adjectives that add letters to their feminine form. Adjectives ending in -teur and -eau/ou. Nous parleronsВ des dГ©marchesВ faites par les artisans franГ§ais afin de protГ©ger la porcelaine de LimogesВ Г  l'Г©chelleВ europГ©enne. Cela nous offriraВ l'occasionВ deВ revenirВ sur l'histoire deВ ce savoir-faireВ franГ§ais. Nous terminerons avec l'expression de la semaine :В Envers et contre tous/tout. Nous verrons que des lieux culturels franГ§ais ont dГ©cidГ© d'augmenterВ leurs tarifs pour les non-EuropГ©ens, ce qui permettra de financer des rГ©novationsВ coГ»teuses.  - La stratГ©gie de sГ©curitГ© nationale amГ©ricaine pour 2025 prГ©ditВ la finВ de l'Europe  - Le Portugal arriveВ en tГӘteВ duВ classementВ 2025 des pays les plus riches  - Des scientifiques dГ©couvrent cinqВ phasesВ dans le dГ©veloppement duВ cerveau humain  - Cloud Dancer,В une nuance de blanc,В est Г©lueВ couleur de l'annГ©e  - Les artisansВ demandentВ la protection de la porcelaine de Limoges Г  l'Г©chelle europГ©enne.  - Des lieux culturels augmentent leurs tarifs pour les non-EuropГ©ens

title: News In Slow Italian #674- Intermediate Italian Weekly Program
summary: IniziamoВ la nostra rassegna di attualitГ В con una discussione sul nuovo documento della Strategia di Sicurezza Nazionale,В pubblicatoВ la settimana scorsa.В AnalizzeremoВ gli aspetti che hannoВ un impatto direttoВ sull'Europa,В nonchГ©В la loro previsione diВ una "cancellazione della civiltГ " europea.В ProseguiremoВ con una conversazione sullaВ classificaВ annuale che valuta 36 economie avanzate sulla base di inflazione,В crescitaВ delВ PIL,В occupazioneВ eВ andamento del mercato azionario. La nostra sezione scientifica ГЁ dedicata a uno studio che ha identificato cinque epoche chiaveВ nello sviluppo del cervello umano. Lo studioВ suggerisceВ quattroВ snodiВ fondamentali a 9, 32, 66 e 83 anni cheВ segnanoВ cambiamenti significativi nell'organizzazione neurale. E l'ultima notizia di attualitГ  riguarda il Pantone, che ha presentato il Colore dell'Anno 2026. PuГІ la scelta di un Colore dell'AnnoВ risultare controversa?  В   La seconda parte della puntata ГЁ dedicata alla lingua e alla cultura italiana.В L'argomento grammaticaleВ di oggi ГЁВ Composite Adverbs.В Ne trovereteВ diversi esempiВ nel dialogo che racconta un caso cheВ sta dividendoВ l'Italia: la storia di una famigliaВ stranieraВ cheВ ha scelto diВ vivere nel bosco, in condizioniВ piuttostoВ precarie, e della decisione del tribunale di sospendere la loro responsabilitГ  genitoriale. Nel finaleВ ci soffermeremoВ sull'espressione idiomatica di oggi:В Non sapere che pesci pigliare. Nel dialogoВ in cui la useremoВ si discute della decisione del Comune di Firenze diВ bandireВ la circolazione deiВ monopattini elettrici a noleggio, a causa del loro utilizzoВ spessoВ irresponsabile,В scorrettoВ eВ pericoloso.  - La Strategia di Sicurezza NazionaleВ degli Stati UnitiВ per il 2025В avverte dellaВ fine dell'Europa  - Il PortogalloВ ГЁ stato incoronatoВ economia dell'anno 2025  - Scienziati individuanoВ cinque epoche distinteВ nello sviluppo del cervello umano  - Cloud Dancer,В una tonalitГ  di bianco, ГЁ il colore dell'anno  - La famigliaВ nel bosco,В una sceltaВ che divide l'Italia  - Firenze dice basta ai monopattini a noleggio

title: News in Slow German - #492 - German Grammar, News and Expressions
summary: Wir beginnen den ersten Teil des Programms mit einer Diskussion Гјber ein neues Dokument zur nationalen Sicherheitsstrategie der USA, das letzte Woche verГ¶ffentlicht wurde. Wir sprechen Гјber die Punkte des Dokuments, die direkte Auswirkungen auf Europa haben, sowie Гјber die im Dokument enthaltene Vorhersage einer вҖһzivilisatorischen AuslГ¶schung" Europas. AnschlieГҹend diskutieren wir Гјber eine jГӨhrliche Rangliste von 36 IndustrielГӨndern, die auf Inflation, Wachstum des Bruttoinlandprodukts, BeschГӨftigung und Aktienmarktperformance basiert. In unserem Wissenschaftsthema geht es heute um eine Studie, die fГјnf verschiedene Phasen in der Entwicklung des menschlichen Gehirns identifiziert hat. Die Studie benennt vier kritische Wendepunkte im Alter von 9, 32, 66 und 83 Jahren, die signifikante VerГӨnderungen in der Strukturierung des Nervensystems markieren. Und zum Schluss diskutieren wir Гјber die Pantone-Farbe des Jahres 2026. Kann die Wahl der Farbe des Jahres kontrovers sein?  Der Rest des Programms ist der deutschen Sprache und Kultur gewidmet. Die heutige Grammatiklektion konzentriert sich auf Verbs Meaning вҖһTo Stop". Es geht um eine musikwissenschaftliche Arbeit, in deren Rahmen zwei neue OrgelstГјcke von Johann Sebastian Bach aufgetaucht sind. Die Spur begann in BrГјssel und verlief Гјber einen SchГјler von Bach. Am Ende wurden die StГјcke in Leipzig uraufgefГјhrt. FГјr Deutsche ist Mallorca schon immer eine Sehnsuchtsdestination gewesen, ein Eldorado. Da Ein Eldorado sein unsere Redewendung fГјr diese Woche ist, haben wir Mallorca, das inoffizielle 17. Bundesland, auch als Thema gewГӨhlt, um den Gebrauch dieser Redewendung zu demonstrieren.   Die neue nationale Sicherheitsstrategie der USA warnt vor dem Ende Europas Portugal ist die вҖһWirtschaft des Jahres" 2025 Die fГјnf verschiedenen Entwicklungsphasen des Gehirns Pantone-Farbe des Jahres: der WeiГҹton вҖһCloud Dancer" Neue Musik von Bach aufgetaucht Mallorca, das 17. deutsche Bundesland

title: News in Slow Spanish - #874 - Intermediate Spanish Weekly Program
summary: Comenzaremos nuestro repaso a la actualidad discutiendo un nuevo documento de Estrategia Nacional de Seguridad publicado la semana pasada. Comentaremos los aspectos del documento que afectan directamente a Europa, asГӯ como la predicciГіn de un "borrado" de la civilizaciГіn europea. Continuaremos hablando de la clasificaciГіn anual de 36 economГӯas desarrolladas en base a la inflaciГіn, el crecimiento del PIB, el empleo y la progresiГіn del mercado bursГЎtil. La secciГіn de ciencia estarГЎ dedicada a un estudio que identificГі cinco periodos del desarrollo del cerebro humano. El estudio sugiere la existencia de cuatro puntos de inflexiГіn cruciales a los 9, 32, 66 y 83 aГұos de edad, que marcan cambios significativos en la organizaciГіn neuronal. Y la Гәltima noticia de actualidad tratarГЎ sobre Pantone, que dio a conocer su Color del AГұo 2026. ВҝPuede la elecciГіn de un Color del AГұo ser polГ©mica?  El resto del episodio de hoy lo dedicaremos a la lengua y la cultura espaГұolas. La primera conversaciГіn incluirГЎ ejemplos del tema de gramГЎtica de la semana, el tiempo verbal Present Perfect - Part III. En esta conversaciГіn hablaremos del mercado laboral en EspaГұa. La sociedad espaГұola ha cambiado mucho. En los aГұos 70, el objetivo de cualquier persona era trabajar en la misma empresa hasta la jubilaciГіn. Hoy en dГӯa, ВЎla prioridad es muy distinta! Y, en nuestra Гәltima conversaciГіn, aprenderemos a usar una nueva expresiГіn espaГұola, Lavarse las manos. La usaremos para hablar del cineasta espaГұol, Luis BuГұuel. Especialmente recordaremos una de sus mГЎs famosas pelГӯculas: El ГЎngel exterminador. Esta pelГӯcula ha sido considerada por el New York Times como una de las mejores 1000 pelГӯculas de toda la historia del cine.   La Estrategia de Seguridad Nacional 2025 de EE. UU. advierte del fin de Europa Portugal, coronado como la economГӯa del aГұo 2025 Los cientГӯficos descubren cinco perГӯodos distintos de desarrollo cerebral Cloud Dancer, una tonalidad del blanco, escogido color del aГұo El mercado laboral en EspaГұa Una de las mil mejores pelГӯculas de la historia del cine

title: Expression: A Shot in the Dark
summary: In todayвҖҷs episode, we break down the common English expressionВ вҖңa shot in the dark,вҖқВ which describes taking a guess or making an attempt with little to no information. YouвҖҷll hear where the idiom comes from, how people from the U.S. use it, and three real-life examplesвҖ”from kids guessing ages, to ordering pintxos in Spain, to recreating Banana KahlГәa French toast (with no instructions!). WeвҖҷll also go over the joke of the day (вҖңWhy did the man take a ladder to the bar?вҖқ), cover some advanced terms and phrases,  and finish with a pronunciation exercise focused on flap Ts. I highly recommend practicing them to  sound more natural when speaking. At the end, IвҖҷll challenge you to think of a moment whenВ youВ took a shot in the dark. title: 226: Frases para sonar mГЎs natural en espaГұol

title: Speak Better English with Harry | Episode 565
summary: In this podcast episode, we look at useful English collocations for talking about economic trends, financial changes, and market movements. You will hear clear explanations that show how native speakers describe rises, falls, stability, and sudden shifts in data. The examples are simple and practical, making it easy to understand how these expressions work in real reports, news stories, and business conversations. This lesson is ideal for English learners who want to improve Business English, understand financial news more easily, and prepare for exams such as IELTS, TOEFL, or Cambridge English. By the end of the episode, you will feel more confident describing changes, analysing trends, and using natural English in professional contexts. title: La Tienda en Cada Esquina de MГ©xico - The Store on Every Corner of Mexico
summary: We dive into the surprising reach of Oxxo, from its 22,000+ stores across Mexico to its rapid growth in countries like Colombia, Chile, Peru, and Brazil. We discuss the 2,500 products you can find in a single location, the ready-to-eat foods, and the essential services it offersвҖ”like paying bills, withdrawing cash, buying bus tickets, and even handling government payments. We also explore Spin by Oxxo, its growing financial service thatвҖҷs helping millions of people access convenient banking tools. By the end of the episode, youвҖҷll understand why Oxxo is more than a convenience storeвҖ”itвҖҷs a powerhouse woven into the daily rhythm of Mexican life. Key Takeaways: Why Oxxo is considered one of the most important convenience stores in Mexico.How its everyday servicesвҖ”from bill payments to cash depositsвҖ”make life easier for millions.The surprising products, expansion, and financial tools behind OxxoвҖҷs massive success.Relevant Links And Additional Resources: 264 вҖ“ Medicina Extremadamente Barata en MГ©xico | Extremely Affordable Medicine in MexicoLevel up your Spanish with our Podcast MembershipGet the full transcript of each episode so you donвҖҷt miss a wordListen to an extended breakdown section in English going over the most important words and phrasesTest your comprehension with a multiple choice quizSupport the show

title: Who Was Ramanujan? The Greatest Mathematician of All Time? (English Vocabulary Lesson)
summary: Today I want to talk about one of the greatest mathematicians of all time. A genius who, with almost no formal training, made mathematical discoveries that still astonish experts today. His name is Srinivasa Ramanujan, and his story is as extraordinary as the mathematical formulas he created. Despite growing up in poverty and facing enormous challenges, Ramanujan produced ideas that continue to shape modern mathematics. This episode is part of my Greatest Scientists Series, where we explore the lives and contributions of people who transformed our understanding of the world (while learning some new English vocabulary). And today, I want to ask an interesting question: can a mathematician like Ramanujan be considered a scientist? Over the next few minutes, weвҖҷll explore his early life in India, his journey to England, the famous collaboration with G.H. Hardy, his remarkable discoveries, his legacy, and finally, weвҖҷll reflect on the role of mathematics itself in the world of science. title: Slow English Podcast: Advanced Listening Practice & Natural Conversation (Comprehensible Input)
summary: Start speaking English TODAY: https://www.skool.com/misshoneysclassroom/about This episode is for upper-intermediate and advanced English learners who want natural, real conversationрҹҘ° Tell me in the commentsрҹ‘ҮрҹҸҪ What were your achievements this year? What are you grateful for? And how do you feel about regrets? LetвҖҷs look back at the highs, lows, lessons, and changes from 2025вңЁ вҖ”starting my TikTok, creating this YouTube channel, traveling, making mistakes, learning from them, and finding meaning in all of it. title: La aventura de la vida, parte 7
summary: XibalbГЎ, the mayan underworld also known as вҖңthe place of fearвҖқ, is divided into rooms. Sandra and Gabriel, joined by the evil men hunting them, have already confronted the rooms of gloom, ice, and knives. Now they must make it through the rooms of bats and games without figuratively - or literally - losing their heads. This story is in the third person and present tense, with multiple other verb forms used as needed to tell the story. Important vocabulary in the story includes: вҖңmurciГ©lagoвҖқ (bat),вҖңruidoвҖқ (noise), вҖңsonidoвҖқ (sound), вҖңpelotaвҖқ (ball), вҖңcanchaвҖқ (court) вҖңchillarвҖқ (to cry), вҖңcabezaвҖқ (head) and вҖңperderвҖқ (to lose). No matter where you are in your language journey, stories will help you on your way. You can find a transcript of the story and read along at https://smalltownspanishteacher.com/2025/12/08/simple-stories-in-spanish-la-aventura-de-la-vida-parte-7/В  Find part 1 here: https://smalltownspanishteacher.com/2025/09/02/simple-stories-in-spanish-la-aventura-de-la-vida-parte-1/В  Find part 2 here: https://smalltownspanishteacher.com/2025/09/22/simple-stories-in-spanish-la-aventura-de-la-vida-parte-2/В  Find part 3 here: https://smalltownspanishteacher.com/2025/10/13/simple-stories-in-spanish-la-aventura-de-la-vida-parte-3/ Find part 4 here: https://smalltownspanishteacher.com/2025/10/26/simple-stories-in-spanish-la-aventura-de-la-vida-parte-4/ Find part 5 here: https://smalltownspanishteacher.com/2025/11/09/simple-stories-in-spanish-la-aventura-de-la-vida-parte-5/В  Find part 6 here: https://smalltownspanishteacher.com/2025/11/24/simple-stories-in-spanish-la-aventura-de-la-vida-parte-6/ ВЎMuchas gracias por escuchar! title: 198: Un tesoro in cantina.
podcast_name: Easy Italian: Learn Italian with real conversations | Imparare l'italiano con conversazioni reali
summary: Iniziamo subito a bacchettare gli italiani oggi, ma poi andiamo subito a fare un giro nella cantina di Matteo! Pronti? Bevanda calda e prendete posto, stiamo per iniziare. Trascrizione interattiva e Vocab Helper Support Easy Italian and get interactive transcripts, live vocabulary and bonus content: easyitalian.fm/membership Note dell'episodio Why You ShouldnвҖҷt Do This Gesture in Italy - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=C5yrp96Kx7w&t=1s Lo sapevi?  A quanto pare in Italia l'inglese si parla male. Ottimo! Cosi' tutti quelli che stanno imparando l'italiano potreanno piu' facilmente parlate italiano invece dell'inglese. Ma dove si parla di piu' e dove si parla di meno? A quanto pare al sud si parla peggio, notizia ancora migliore, piu' sole e piu' mare! Ma perche' si parla poco inglese e si impara male? Matteo e Raffaele fanno "due chiacchiere" e cercano di capire cosa non va con l'inglese e gli italiani.  Torniamo poi in Italia, Milano, casa di Matteo e Katie. Precisamente in cantina. Matteo facendo un po' di ordine e cercando di sistemare un po' la cantina ha trovato qualcosa.. Ma cosa? Concludiamo con un "incidente" interessante che riguarda alcuni italiani e "Mediaworld". Cosa e' successo? Prezzi pazzi per il black friday! Forse troppo pazzi? Trascrizione Raffaele: [0:23] Good morning, Matteo! Matteo: [0:27] Buongiorno, come siamo internazionali! Raffaele: [0:31] E sai, sono tornato dall'Inghilterra, parte di me ГЁ ancora lГ¬ e poi ho letto una notizia che mi ha fatto mettere le mani nei capelli. Matteo: [0:40] Ah, e allora andiamo, lanciamoci subito a bomba, come diceva un mio professore d'italiano. Lo sapevi? Raffaele: [0:56] Lo sapevi cosa vuol dire "good morning", Matteo? ... title: Simplified Speech #234 вҖ“ The Holidays 2025
summary: In this Chatterbox episode, Andrew and Anna talk about what curiosity is, why you feel it, and how it can help or hurt you in everyday life. You will hear real stories about things they have been curious about lately, how curiosity can help you discover new ideas, and how it can sometimes distract you. They also explain the different types of curiosity (diversive curiosity and specific curiosity) and share useful idioms related to curiosity like вҖңcuriosity killed the cat.вҖқ What you will learn in this episode: Natural English phrases to describe what you want to learn, what interests you, and what catches your attention. Common words and idioms connected to curiosity, such as вҖңchildlike curiosity,вҖқ вҖңhealthy curiosity,вҖқ and вҖңmy curiosity got the better of me.вҖқ How Andrew and Anna talk about what interests them, respond to each otherвҖҷs stories, and describe curiosity in clear, everyday English. This episode is perfect for you if: You want to think in English more easily when talking about what interests you You want to sound natural and confident when sharing your own stories and questions You want to learn clear, useful expressions to describe curiosity in everyday life The Best Way to Learn with This Episode: Culips members get an interactive transcript, helpful study guide, and ad-free audio for this episode. title: News In Slow French #771- French Course with Current Events
summary: Pour commencer,В nous aborderonsВ un sujet sГ©rieux.В Les dГ©penses militairesВ des pays europГ©ensВ ont augmentГ©.В Combien de tempsВ reste-t-il Г  l'UE pour se prГ©parer Г В une Г©ventuelle agressionВ de la Russie ?В Nous enchaГ®neronsВ avec une discussion surВ le projetВ de l'Union europГ©enne d'assouplir les rГЁglesВ en matiГЁre deВ subventions publiquesВ pourВ promouvoirВ le logement social. Comment l'UEВ compte-t-elle y parvenirВ ? Dans notre section scientifique, nous parlerons d'une Г©tudeВ qui a conclu que les enfantsВ arrivent beaucoup mieuxВ que les adultes Г  imiter les peintures de l'expressionniste abstrait Jackson Pollock. Et pour conclure la premiГЁre partie de l'Г©mission, nous parlerons d'un nouveau В«В crime du siГЁcleВ В» : 90 000 euros d'escargotsВ ont Г©tГ© volГ©sВ Г В un Г©leveurВ franГ§ais.  В   Le reste de l'Г©mission d'aujourd'hui sera consacrГ© Г  la langue et Г  la culture franГ§aises.В Notre point de grammaireВ de la semaine sera :В Les adjectifs se terminant par -et, -ot, -s, -x, -f et -eur. Nous nous intГ©resserons au couple cГ©lГЁbre de pandas du zoo de Beauval qui viennent de quitter la France pour retourner en Chine etВ nous verronsВ pourquoi ces animaux sont de vГ©ritables stars. Nous terminerons avec l'expression de la semaine,В Faire / Dire des messes basses. Nous parlerons deВ l'annulationВ de la 53e Г©dition du festival deВ bande dessinГ©eВ d'AngoulГӘme, le plus grand au monde, quiВ devait se tenirВ en janvier.  - Face Г  la menace russe, l'EuropeВ augmenteВ considГ©rablementВ ses dГ©penses militaires  - L'UEВ lutte contreВ la crise du logementВ en assouplissant les rГЁglesВ et en proposantВ un soutien financier  - La critique de l'art moderne qu'on entendВ le plus courammentВ est-elle fondГ©e ?  - Un Г©leveurВ franГ§aisВ se fait volerВ pour 90 000 euros d'escargots  - Les pandas du zoo de Beauval retournent en Chine  - Annulation de la prochaine Г©dition du festival de bande dessinГ©e d'AngoulГӘme

title: News In Slow Italian #673- Italian News, Grammar, and Expressions
summary: InizieremoВ da una notizia piuttosto seria.В La spesa militareВ dei Paesi europeiВ ГЁ aumentata. Quanto tempo resta all'UE per prepararsi a una potenziale aggressione da parte della Russia?В Proseguiremo conВ una discussione sui piani dell'UE perВ allentareВ le rigide normeВ sugliВ aiutiВ di Stato, con l'obiettivo di favorireВ l'edilizia abitativa a prezzi accessibili. Cosa intende fare l'UE per raggiungere questo obiettivo? Nel nostro segmento scientificoВ parleremo diВ uno studioВ secondo cuiВ i bambini riescono a imitareВ i dipintiВ dell'espressionista astrattoВ Jackson Pollock molto meglio degli adulti. E per concludere la prima parte della puntata, parleremo dell'ennesimoВ "colpo del secolo":В il furtoВ di escargot per un valore di 90.000 euro ai danni di un allevatore francese.  В   La seconda parte dellaВ puntataВ ГЁ dedicata alla lingua e alla cultura italiana.В L'argomento grammaticaleВ di oggi ГЁВ Simple Adverbs.В Ne trovereteВ diversi esempi nel dialogo dedicato alla memoria di una figuraВ scomparsa di recenteВ e cheВ ha segnato profondamenteВ la storia della musica italiana: Ornella Vanoni. Nel finaleВ ci soffermeremoВ sull'espressione idiomatica di oggi:В Tastare il terrenoВ . Il dialogo in cuiВ la useremoВ racconta il successo della nazionale maschile alla Coppa Davis, il torneo piГ№ importante al mondo perВ le squadre maschili. Una vittoria che arriva dopo quelle degli ultimi due anni e che scriveВ un capitolo significativoВ nella storia di questo sport.  - Di fronte all'aggressione russa, l'EuropaВ aumenta drasticamente la sua spesa militare  - L'UEВ affronta la crisi abitativaВ allentando le regoleВ eВ offrendoВ nuoviВ aiuti finanziari  - La critica piГ№ banale all'arte moderna haВ un fondo di veritГ ?  - Ladri derubanoВ un allevatore francese di escargotВ per un valore di 90.000 euro  - Addio a Ornella Vanoni,В la voce che ha segnatoВ la musica italiana  - Tennis, l'Italia riscrive la storia della Coppa Davis

title: #582 | Piazza Fontana & The Accidental Death of An Anarchist
summary: A deadly bomb. A death in a police station. A secret kept from the public.В  In 1969, an explosion in Milan killed 17 people. The police accused a group of anarchists, but the story was not that simple.В  In part one of this three-part mini-series on "Gli Anni Di Piombo" (The Years of Lead), we look at how this tragedy started one of the darkest times in Italian history.В  Italy modernising fast, but divided and tense.12 December 1969: bomb at MilanвҖҷs Piazza Fontana.Seventeen dead, eighty-eight injured; shock across Italy.Coordinated Rome bombs; no claim; anarchists blamed.Anarchist Giuseppe Pinelli dies after police questioning; controversy.Pietro Valpreda arrested; weak evidence; years before acquittal.Evidence pointed to far-right group Ordine Nuovo.Suspected cover-ups by police and secret services.Strategy of tension; suspected Gladio links; extent unclear.Impact: fear, ordinary victims, start of the Years of Lead.В Full interactive transcript, subtitles and key vocabulary available on the website: https://www.leonardoenglish.com/podcasts/piazza-fontana --- You might like: рҹ”“ Unlock bonus episodes, interactive transcripts, subtitles & vocabulary lists вңЁ 5 Powerful Tips for Learning English with Podcasts (Faster & Effectively!) title: News in Slow German - #491 - Intermediate German Weekly Program
summary: Wir beginnen das Programm mit einem ernsten Thema. Die MilitГӨrausgaben der europГӨischen LГӨnder wurden erhГ¶ht. Wie viel Zeit bleibt der EU noch, um sich auf einen mГ¶glichen Angriff Russlands vorzubereiten? AnschlieГҹend diskutieren wir Гјber die PlГӨne der EU, die strengen Regeln fГјr staatliche Beihilfen zu lockern, um den Bau von erschwinglichem Wohnraum zu fГ¶rdern. Wie will die EU das erreichen? In unserem Wissenschaftsthema sprechen wir heute Гјber eine Studie, die zeigt, dass Kinder GemГӨlde des abstrakten Expressionisten Jackson Pollock wesentlich besser nachahmen kГ¶nnen als Erwachsene. Und zum Abschluss des ersten Teils des Programms sprechen wir Гјber ein weiteres вҖһJahrhundertverbrechen": Einem franzГ¶sischen SchneckenzГјchter wurden Weinbergschnecken im Wert von 90.000 Euro gestohlen.  Der Rest des Programms ist der deutschen Sprache und Kultur gewidmet. Die heutige Grammatiklektion konzentriert sich auf Flavoring Particles (Modalpartikel) вҖ“ Part 3. Es geht um einen Rechtsstreit zwischen der GEMA, einer Organisation, die die Interessen von KГјnstlern in Deutschland vertritt, und der KI-Firma OpenAI. Ausgangspunkt war, dass OpenAI urheberrechtlich geschГјtzte Texte deutscher Musiker zum Training seines Programms ChatGPT genutzt hatte, ohne dafГјr zu zahlen. Ein Gericht urteilte nun zugunsten der KГјnstler. Unsere Redewendung der Woche ist Sich einen hinter die Binde kippen. Nichts ist besser geeignet, um den Gebrauch dieser Redewendung zu veranschaulichen, als die KГ¶lner und ihr heiГҹgeliebtes Bier вҖ“ das KГ¶lsch. Ein Besuch der Stadt sollte nicht enden, ohne sich zumindest ein KГ¶lsch hinter die Binde gekippt zu haben.   Drastische ErhГ¶hung der MilitГӨrausgaben in Europa angesichts eines mГ¶glichen russischen Angriffs EU reagiert auf Wohnraumkrise mit Lockerung der Vorschriften und mehr finanzieller UnterstГјtzung Ist moderne Kunst ein Kinderspiel? Schneckenraub in Frankreich: Diebe stehlen Weinbergschnecken im Wert von 90.000 Euro Streit um das Urheberrecht bei KI-Musik Die KГ¶lner und ihr KГ¶lsch

title: News in Slow Spanish - #873 - Learn Spanish through Current Events
summary: La primera parte del programa la comenzaremos con una noticia seria. El gasto militar de los paГӯses europeos ha aumentado. ВҝCuГЎnto tiempo le queda a la UE para prepararse para un potencial ataque de Rusia? Continuaremos discutiendo los planes de la UE para flexibilizar las estrictas normas que regulan la ayuda a los paГӯses miembro, con el objetivo de impulsar la oferta de vivienda asequible. ВҝCГіmo planea lograrlo la UE? En la secciГіn de ciencia, hablaremos de un estudio que asegura que los niГұos pueden imitar los cuadros del pintor expresionista Jackson Pollock mucho mejor que los adultos. Y, para concluir la primera parte del programa de hoy, hablaremos de otro "crГӯmen del siglo": el robo de caracoles por valor de 90.000 euros a un granjero francГ©s.  El resto del episodio de hoy lo dedicaremos a la lengua y la cultura espaГұolas. La primera conversaciГіn incluirГЎ ejemplos del tema de gramГЎtica de la semana, el tiempo verbal Present Perfect - Part II. En esta conversaciГіn hablaremos del Templo Expiatorio de la Sagrada Familia de Barcelona. El arquitecto de la BasГӯlica fue Antonio GaudГӯ, e ideГі un proyecto tan grande, que pensГі no poderse terminar en mucho tiempo. Sin embargo, las obras ya estГЎn llegando a la recta final... Y, en nuestra Гәltima conversaciГіn, aprenderemos a usar una nueva expresiГіn espaГұola, No es oro todo lo que reluce. La usaremos para hablar de la llegada de CristГіbal ColГіn a AmГ©rica en 1492, con las ventajas y desventajas que esa colonizaciГіn trajo, tanto para los pueblos indГӯgenas, como para los colonizadores espaГұoles.   Entre la agresividad rusa, Europa aumenta drГЎsticamente el gasto militar La UE combate la crisis de la vivienda relajando las normas y ofreciendo apoyo econГіmico ВҝEs la crГӯtica mГЎs banal del arte moderno correcta? Unos ladrones le roban a un granjero francГ©s caracoles por valor de 90.000 euros BasГӯlica de la Sagrada Familia El "descubrimiento" de AmГ©rica y el auge del Imperio

(https://www.easyfrench.fm/132) Easy French Podcast 153: Quand tu ne progresses plus en franГ§ais (https://www.easyfrench.fm/153) Playlist "CrГ©tin de cerveau", chaГ®ne YouTube Science Etonnante (https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLxzM9a5lhAumFRpcigmGY1QLDYxb4-P2B) Transcript Intro Judith: [0:16] Bonjour les amis, bienvenue dans un nouvel Г©pisode du podcast Easy French. Et bonjour Г  toi HГ©lГЁne. HГ©lГЁne: [0:23] Salut, comment Г§a va ? Judith: [0:26] ГҮa va plutГҙt bien. Et toi ? HГ©lГЁne: [0:29] Moi aussi, j'ai bien mangГ©. Il y a du soleil, on peut dire que tout va bien. Judith: [0:35] Ah bah super. Cette semaine, il y a l'Г©pisode sur les voisins qui sort sur YouTube. HГ©lГЁne: [0:39] Ouais. Judith: [0:40] Et c'est drГҙle parce que mes voisins du dessous ont un petit dГ©gГўt des eaux, une petite fuite. Et Г§a vient probablement de chez moi, mГӘme si moi, je ne constate rien chez moi. Mais c'est la premiГЁre fois que je suis confrontГ©e Г  ce genre de problГЁme d'adulte. Et qu'il faut contacter les assurances et que c'est Г  n'y plus rien comprendre. HГ©lГЁne: [1:00] Oh lГ  lГ , je dГ©teste ce genre de problГЁme. Judith: [1:02] ГҮa t'est dГ©jГ  arrivГ© d'avoir une fuite ou d'avoir peut-ГӘtre causГ© une fuite ? HГ©lГЁne: [1:06] Ouais, je crois, ouais. Judith: [1:07] Si tu veux, je te tiendrai au courant de l'avancement de cette affaire. HГ©lГЁne: [1:11] Avec plaisir. Judith: [1:13] Et avant d'attaquer le sujet de la semaine, je te propose d'Г©couter le message d'un abonnГ© qui s'appelle Vlad. HГ©lГЁne: [1:20] Allez ! Message de Vlad Vlad: [1:21] Bonjour Judith et HГ©lГЁne, je m'appelle Vlad, je vis aux Гүtats-Unis et je viens de dГ©couvrir votre podcast et je l'aime beaucoup. J'ai une petite question. Il y a une phrase que vous disiez beaucoup dans le podcast, je pense, ou assez souvent. Je pense que c'est surtout Judith qui la disait, c'est mine de rien. Est-ce que vous pouvez m'expliquer cette phrase ? Parce que je ne capte pas. Merci. HГ©lГЁne: [1:54] Alors, c'est une super question qu'on nous a dГ©jГ  posГ©e. Et Г  chaque fois que je dois traduire cette expression en anglais, j'ai un problГЁme. C'est super dur Г  traduire. Judith: [2:08] Ah oui, je n'avais pas pensГ© Г  la traduire, mais j'avais pensГ© Г ... Qu'est-ce qu'on pourrait dire Г  la place en franГ§ais ? HГ©lГЁne: [2:13] C'est tellement idiomatique. Je ne vois pas ce qu'on pourrait dire Г  la place parce que dГ©jГ , Г§a n'a pas beaucoup de sens. Judith: [2:21] C'est vrai. Mine de rien, Г§a n'a pas beaucoup de sens en soi. title: 225: ВҝPor quГ© te enfermaste, Pau?
podcast_name: Easy Spanish: Learn Spanish with everyday conversations | Conversaciones del dГӯa a dГӯa para aprender espaГұol
summary: La llegada de diciembre es un momento crГӯtico en el cual nos enfermamos casi todos... Вҝverdad? Y el equipo de Easy Spanish no es una excepciГіn рҹҳ…. Al volver a MГ©xico, Pau se siente un poco enferma y, junto con Carla, aprovechan para hablar sobre por quГ© nos enfermamos tanto. Desde las toses y gripes hasta los piojos... ВЎAl doctor! Easy Spanish Community Al unirte a la comunidad de Easy Spanish puedes llevar tu experiencia de aprendizaje al siguiente nivel. Los miembros de nuestra Podcast Membership reciben: Vocab Helper: El vocabulario mГЎs importante de cada minuto del podcast directamente en la pantalla de tu celular Interactive transcript: Una transcripciГіn interactiva donde podrГЎs leer y escuchar el podcast al mismo tiempo, con una funciГіn de traducciГіn en tiempo real Exclusive aftershow: DespuГ©s de cada episodio, Pau y JosГ© discuten un poquito mГЎs sobre el tema desde un punto de vista un poco mГЎs personal. Discord community: Acceso a la comunidad en Discord de Easy Spanish, donde puedes hablar con los miembros de nuestro equipo y otras personas que, como tГә, se encuentran en la aventura de aprender nuestro idioma Extra content for our YouTube episodes: Hojas de ejercicios, listas de vocabulario y transcripciones de todos nuestros episodios de YouTube. Si todavГӯa no eres miembro de la comunidad de Easy Spanish, puedes unirte en easy-spanish.org/community (https://www.easy-spanish.org/community) EnvГӯanos un mensaje de audio ВЎYa puedes enviarnos mensajes de audio para que los escuchemos en el podcast! Para hacerlo tienes que ir a easyspanish.fm (https://www.easyspanish.fm) y dar clic en el botГіn amarillo que aparecerГЎ a la derecha de la pГЎgina.  TranscripciГіn Paulina: [0:03] Hola, Carla. Carla: [0:04] Hola, Pau. Paulina: [0:06] Al mismo tiempo, exactamente. Carla: [0:10] Y porque no estaba preparado, Pau. Que, si lo preparamos, nos sale mal. Paulina: [0:15] Exacto. Que si lo hubiГ©ramos querido hacer, no nos salГӯa. ВҝCГіmo estГЎs, Carla? Carla: [0:23] Estoy ya entrando prГЎcticamente en el espГӯritu navideГұo porque llevo ya mi bata, he encendido la calefacciГіn porque hace frГӯo. AsГӯ que estoy muy contenta, Pau. Paulina: [0:35] Pues, ya es diciembre y empieza este tiempo, Вҝno? Carla: [0:38] AsГӯ es. Y pienso que tГә tambiГ©n has notado que es diciembre porque te noto la voz como cogida. title: Speak Better English with Harry | Episode 564
summary: In this podcast episode, we explore common English idioms connected to music and everyday situations. You will learn how native speakers use these expressions naturally in conversations, stories, and informal speech. Each one is explained in simple English with clear context so you can understand the meaning, the tone, and when it is appropriate to use it. This lesson is ideal for intermediate and advanced English learners who want to expand vocabulary, sound more fluent, and prepare for exams like IELTS, TOEFL, or Cambridge English. By the end, you will feel more confident recognising and using music-related idioms in both professional and casual conversations. title: Des noms de femmes scientifiques sur la tour EiffelВ : rencontre avec BГ©atrice Marticorena

summary: Dans quelques jours, il y aura une annonce Г  propos de la tour Eiffel ! title: La Cocina Ancestral OaxaquenМғa con Evangelina - The Ancient Oaxacan Kitchen with Evangelina
summary: May talks with Evangelina about the origins of Cocina Nana Vira and how a challenging moment in her familyвҖҷs life transformed into a project that now welcomes visitors from around the world. She shares the cultural meaning and emotional depth behind OaxacaвҖҷs ceremonial molesвҖ”coloradito, mole negro, and mole chichiloвҖ”and explains why traditional tools like the metate and fire-based cooking are essential to preserving authentic Zapotec flavors. We also bring listeners into the heart of our Oaxaca immersion experience, where students explore local markets, learn regional traditions, and cook alongside Evangelina in her wood-fired kitchen. Key Takeaways: The powerful story behind Cocina Nana Vira and how EvangelinaвҖҷs family revived ancestral cooking traditions.The cultural significance of OaxacaвҖҷs ceremonial moles and what makes each one unique.Why traditional tools, native ingredients, and fire-based techniques are essential to preserving Zapotec culinary heritage.Relevant Links And Additional Resources: 158 вҖ“ TГ©cnicas Ancestrales En La Cocina Mexicana Parte 2 | Ancient Techniques In Mexican Cuisine Part 2157 вҖ“ TГ©cnicas Ancestrales En La Cocina Mexicana Parte 1 | Ancient Techniques In Mexican Cuisine Part 1Boost your confidence in real-life Spanish conversations with our Spanish Immersion RetreatsLevel up your Spanish with our Podcast MembershipGet the full transcript of each episode so you donвҖҷt miss a wordListen to an extended breakdown section in English going over the most important words and phrasesTest your comprehension with a multiple choice quizSupport the show
